instances.inspect_output:
**OBJECTIVE**  
The purpose of this study was to characterize the relationship between adipose tissue phenotype and depot-specific microvascular function in fat.

**METHODS AND RESULTS**  
In 30 obese subjects (age 42±11 years, body mass index 46±11 kg/m²) undergoing bariatric surgery, we intraoperatively collected visceral and subcutaneous adipose tissue and characterized depot-specific adipose phenotypes. We assessed vasomotor function of the adipose microvasculature using videomicroscopy of small arterioles (75-250 μm) isolated from different fat compartments. Endothelium-dependent, acetylcholine-mediated vasodilation was severely impaired in visceral arterioles compared to the subcutaneous depot (P<0.001 by ANOVA). Nonendothelium dependent responses to papaverine and nitroprusside were similar. Endothelial nitric oxide synthase inhibition with N(ω)-nitro-l-arginine methyl ester reduced subcutaneous vasodilation but had no effect on severely blunted visceral arteriolar responses. Visceral fat exhibited greater expression of proinflammatory, oxidative stress-related, hypoxia-induced, and proangiogenic genes; increased activated macrophage populations; and had a higher capacity for cytokine production ex vivo.

**CONCLUSIONS**  
Our findings provide clinical evidence that the visceral microenvironment may be intrinsically toxic to arterial health, providing a potential mechanism by which visceral adiposity burden is linked to atherosclerotic vascular disease. Our findings also support the evolving concept that both adipose tissue quality and quantity may play significant roles in shaping cardiovascular phenotypes in human obesity.
instances.mapper_output: the visceral microenvironment may be intrinsically toxic to arterial health
instances.fs_output: Visceral adipose tissue exhibits impaired microvascular function and a proinflammatory environment, linking it to cardiovascular disease in obesity.
instances.control_output:
The study aimed to explore the relationship between adipose tissue characteristics and the function of microvasculature in different fat depots. In a cohort of 30 obese individuals undergoing bariatric surgery, both visceral and subcutaneous adipose tissues were collected and analyzed. The researchers evaluated the vasomotor function of small arterioles from these fat compartments using videomicroscopy.

Key findings included:

- Endothelium-dependent vasodilation, induced by acetylcholine, was significantly impaired in visceral arterioles compared to those from the subcutaneous depot (P<0.001).
- Nonendothelium-dependent vasodilatory responses to agents like papaverine and nitroprusside were comparable between the two fat depots.
- Inhibition of endothelial nitric oxide synthase reduced vasodilation in subcutaneous arterioles but did not affect the already diminished responses in visceral arterioles.
- Visceral fat showed higher expression levels of genes associated with inflammation, oxidative stress, hypoxia, and angiogenesis, along with increased populations of activated macrophages and a greater capacity for cytokine production.

The study concludes that the visceral adipose microenvironment may be detrimental to arterial health, potentially explaining the link between visceral fat accumulation and atherosclerotic vascular disease. It emphasizes the importance of both the quality and quantity of adipose tissue in influencing cardiovascular health in obesity.
instances.soft2_output: Visceral adipose tissue has a unique phenotype that is associated with systemic inflammation in obese humans

instances.input: Globally, about 1% of pregnant women are persistently infected with the hepatitis C virus (HCV). Mother-to-child transmission of HCV occurs in 3-5% of pregnancies and accounts for most new childhood infections. HCV-specific CD8(+) cytotoxic T lymphocytes (CTLs) are vital in the clearance of acute HCV infections, but in the 60-80% of infections that persist, these cells become functionally exhausted or select for mutant viruses that escape T cell recognition. Increased HCV replication during pregnancy suggests that maternofetal immune tolerance mechanisms may further impair HCV-specific CTLs, limiting their selective pressure on persistent viruses. To assess this possibility, we characterized circulating viral quasispecies during and after consecutive pregnancies in two women. This revealed a loss of some escape mutations in HLA class I epitopes during pregnancy that was associated with emergence of more fit viruses. CTL selective pressure was reimposed after childbirth, at which point escape mutations in these epitopes again predominated in the quasispecies and viral load dropped sharply. Importantly, the viruses transmitted perinatally were those with enhanced fitness due to reversion of escape mutations. Our findings indicate that the immunoregulatory changes of pregnancy reduce CTL selective pressure on HCV class I epitopes, thereby facilitating vertical transmission of viruses with optimized replicative fitness.
instances.output: Loss of immune escape mutations during persistent HCV infection in pregnancy enhances replication of vertically transmitted viruses

During pregnancy, there is an increase in HCV replication, which may be linked to maternal-fetal immune tolerance mechanisms that further diminish the effectiveness of CTLs. The research involved analyzing the viral quasispecies in two women during and after consecutive pregnancies. The findings revealed that certain escape mutations in HLA class I epitopes were lost during pregnancy, leading to the emergence of more fit viral variants. After childbirth, CTL selective pressure was restored, resulting in a resurgence of escape mutations and a significant drop in viral load.

Crucially, the study found that the viruses transmitted from mother to child were those that had reverted to a more fit state due to the loss of escape mutations. This suggests that the immunological changes that occur during pregnancy can reduce CTL pressure on HCV, facilitating the vertical transmission of more virulent strains. Overall, the research underscores the complex interplay between maternal immunity and viral evolution during pregnancy.

instances.input: Hematopoietic stem cells (HSCs) develop during embryogenesis in a complex process that involves multiple anatomical sites. Once HSC precursors have been specified from mesoderm, they have to mature into functional HSCs and undergo self-renewing divisions to generate a pool of HSCs. During this process, developing HSCs migrate through various embryonic niches, which provide signals for their establishment and the conservation of their self-renewal ability. These processes have to be recapitulated to generate HSCs from embryonic stem cells. Elucidating the interactions between developing HSCs and their niches should facilitate the generation and expansion of HSCs in vitro to exploit their clinical potential.
instances.output: The journey of developing hematopoietic stem cells.

```python
def count_guitar_players(sentence_A, sentence_B):
    # Initialize the counts
    count_A = 0

        count_A += 1
    if "girl" in sentence_B:
        count_B += 1

    # Return the difference in the counts
    return count_B - count_A
```

```

instances.soft_output: What describes the proportionality of acceleration to force and mass?
instances.inspect_output:
The relationship that describes the proportionality of acceleration to force and mass is Newton's Second Law of Motion. It states that the acceleration (a) of an object is directly proportional to the net force (F) acting on it and inversely proportional to its mass (m). This can be expressed with the formula:

\[ F = m \cdot a \]

This means that if you increase the force applied to an object, its acceleration will increase, and if you increase the mass of the object while keeping the force constant, the acceleration will decrease.

instances.input: How do socialists think the financial assets no the means of production shouldn't be owned?
instances.output: How do socialists think the means of production shouldn't be owned?
instances.soft_output:
assistant

How do socialists think the means of production shouldn't be owned?
instances.inspect_output:
Socialists generally believe that the means of production—such as factories, land, and resources—should not be privately owned by individuals or corporations. Instead, they advocate for collective or public ownership. The rationale behind this belief includes:

1. **Equity and Fairness**: Socialists argue that private ownership leads to inequality, as a small number of individuals can accumulate vast wealth while others struggle. By collectively owning the means of production, resources can be distributed more equitably.

2. **Worker Control**: Socialists emphasize the importance of workers having a say in the production process. They believe that when workers own and manage the means of production, they are more likely to create a fair and just workplace.

3. **Elimination of Exploitation**: In a capitalist system, owners of capital can profit from the labor of workers, which socialists view as exploitative. By removing private ownership, socialists aim to eliminate this exploitation and ensure that workers receive the full value of their labor.

4. **Focus on Social Good**: Socialists argue that production should be oriented towards meeting the needs of society rather than generating profit. Collective ownership allows for planning and production that prioritizes social welfare over individual profit.

5. **Sustainability**: Many socialists advocate for sustainable practices that benefit the environment and society as a whole. They believe that collective ownership can lead to more responsible management of resources.

In summary, socialists believe that financial assets and the means of production should be collectively owned to promote equity, eliminate exploitation, empower workers, and focus on the common good.
instances.mapper_output: How do socialists think the financial assets should be owned?
instances.fs_output: How do socialists think the means of production shouldn't be owned?
instances.control_output: Socialists generally believe that the means of production—such as factories, land, and resources—should not be privately owned because they argue that private ownership leads to exploitation and inequality. Instead, socialists advocate for collective or state ownership of these assets, which they believe would allow for more equitable distribution of wealth and resources. They argue that when the means of production are owned by individuals or corporations, profits are prioritized over the needs of the community, leading to social and economic disparities. By having the means of production owned collectively, socialists aim to ensure that the benefits of production are shared among all members of society, rather than concentrated in the hands of a few.
instances.soft2_output: How do socialists think the means of production shouldn't be owned?

instances.input: What does deterrence no focusing on morality help a judge to achieve?
instances.output: What does focusing on morality help a judge to achieve?
instances.soft_output: assistant Focusing on morality helps a judge to achieve what?
instances.inspect_output:
Deterrence that does not focus on morality helps a judge to achieve several objectives:

1. **Consistency in Sentencing**: By focusing on deterrence rather than moral considerations, judges can apply sentences more uniformly, ensuring that similar offenses receive similar punishments, which promotes fairness in the legal system.

2. **Reduction of Crime Rates**: The primary goal of deterrence is to prevent future crimes. By imposing penalties that are designed to deter individuals from committing offenses, judges can contribute to a decrease in crime rates.

3. **Clarity of Legal Standards**: Deterrence-based sentencing emphasizes the legal consequences of actions, making it clear to the public what behaviors are unacceptable and what the repercussions will be, thus enhancing the rule of law.

4. **Focus on Public Safety**: By prioritizing deterrence, judges can concentrate on protecting society by incapacitating offenders and preventing them from committing further crimes.

5. **Encouragement of Compliance**: A deterrent approach can encourage individuals to comply with the law out of fear of punishment, rather than out of a sense of moral obligation, which can be particularly effective in certain contexts.

6. **Resource Allocation**: By focusing on deterrence, judges can help allocate resources towards crime prevention and law enforcement strategies that are aimed at reducing recidivism and enhancing community safety.

Overall, a deterrence-focused approach allows judges to prioritize the practical implications of their decisions on crime and public safety, rather than getting entangled in moral debates.
instances.mapper_output: What does deterrence no focusing on morality help a judge to achieve?
instances.fs_output: What does deterrence help a judge to achieve?
instances.control_output:
Deterrence that does not focus on morality helps a judge to achieve several objectives:

1. **Consistency in Sentencing**: By prioritizing deterrence over moral considerations, judges can apply sentences more uniformly, ensuring that similar offenses receive similar punishments, which promotes fairness in the legal system.

2. **Reduction of Crime Rates**: The primary goal of deterrence is to discourage criminal behavior. By imposing penalties that are perceived as significant enough to outweigh the benefits of committing a crime, judges can contribute to a decrease in crime rates.

3. **Focus on Public Safety**: Deterrence emphasizes the protection of society by preventing future crimes. Judges can prioritize the safety of the community by imposing sentences that aim to deter not only the individual offender but also potential future offenders.

4. **Objective Decision-Making**: By removing moral considerations, judges can make decisions based on empirical evidence and statistical data regarding the effectiveness of various penalties in deterring crime, leading to more rational and objective outcomes.

5. **Encouragement of Rehabilitation**: While deterrence is often seen as punitive, a focus on deterrence can also lead to the implementation of rehabilitation programs aimed at reducing recidivism, as judges may recognize that addressing underlying issues can be a form of deterrence.

6. **Resource Allocation**: By focusing on deterrence, judges can help allocate resources more effectively within the criminal justice system, directing efforts towards strategies that have been shown to reduce crime rather than those based solely on moral judgments.

Overall, a deterrence-focused approach allows judges to prioritize the practical outcomes of their decisions, aiming for a more effective and efficient justice system.
instances.soft2_output: What does focusing on morality help a judge to achieve?

instances.input: What method or rather function is related to Basel numbers?
instances.output: What function is related to Basel numbers?
instances.soft_output: assistantI don't have information about a function related to Basel numbers. Can I help you with something else?
instances.inspect_output:
The method related to Basel numbers is known as the **Basel problem**, which involves the sum of the reciprocals of the squares of the natural numbers. The specific function associated with this is the **Riemann zeta function**, denoted as ζ(s). For the case of Basel numbers, we are particularly interested in ζ(2), which is equal to:

\[
\zeta(2) = \sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{1}{n^2} = \frac{\pi^2}{6}
\]

This result was famously solved by Leonhard Euler in the 18th century and is a key result in number theory.
instances.mapper_output: What method is related to Basel numbers?
instances.fs_output: What function is related to Basel numbers?
instances.control_output:
The function related to Basel numbers is the Riemann zeta function, specifically evaluated at even positive integers. The Basel problem, which was solved by Leonhard Euler, is concerned with the sum of the reciprocals of the squares of the natural numbers, leading to the result:

\[
\zeta(2) = \sum_{n=1}^{\infty} \frac{1}{n^2} = \frac{\pi^2}{6}
\]

More generally, the Basel numbers are related to the values of the Riemann zeta function at even integers, where:

\[
\zeta(2n) = \frac{(-1)^{n+1} B_{2n} (2\pi)^{2n}}{2(2n)!}
\]

Here, \( B_{2n} \) are the Bernoulli numbers.
instances.soft2_output: What function is related to Basel numbers?

proper question: As of January 2016 how many digits does the largest known prime consist of?

 context: The property of being prime (or not) is called primality. A simple but slow method of verifying the primality of a given number n is known as trial division. It consists of testing whether n is a multiple of any integer between 2 and . Algorithms much more efficient than trial division have been devised to test the primality of large numbers. These include the Miller–Rabin primality test, which is fast but has a small probability of error, and the AKS primality test, which always produces the correct answer in polynomial time but is too slow to be practical. instances.inspect_output:
The main types of analysis completed by the FBI Laboratory include:

1. **Forensic Analysis**: This involves the examination of physical evidence from crime scenes, such as fingerprints, firearms, and trace evidence.

2. **DNA Analysis**: The laboratory conducts DNA testing to identify individuals involved in criminal activities.

3. **Toxicology**: This analysis determines the presence of drugs or poisons in biological samples.

4. **Digital Forensics**: The FBI Laboratory analyzes electronic devices and data to uncover evidence related to cybercrimes and other offenses.

5. **Ballistics**: This involves the examination of firearms and ammunition to link them to specific crimes.

6. **Document Analysis**: The laboratory analyzes questioned documents to verify authenticity or detect alterations.

7. **Explosives Analysis**: This includes the examination of explosive materials and devices to determine their origin and use.

These analyses support criminal investigations and help ensure justice is served.
instances.control_output:
The main types of analysis completed by the FBI Laboratory include:

1. **Forensic Analysis**: This involves the examination of physical evidence from crime scenes, such as fingerprints, firearms, and trace evidence.

2. **DNA Analysis**: The laboratory conducts DNA testing to identify individuals involved in criminal activities.

3. **Toxicology**: Analysis of biological samples to detect the presence of drugs, alcohol, or poisons.

4. **Ballistics**: Examination of firearms and ammunition to determine the source of bullets and cartridge cases.

5. **Digital Forensics**: Recovery and analysis of data from electronic devices, including computers and mobile phones.

6. **Document Analysis**: Examination of questioned documents to verify authenticity or detect alterations.

7. **Explosives Analysis**: Investigation of explosive materials and devices to determine their composition and origin.

8. **Trace Evidence Analysis**: Analysis of small particles or fibers that may link a suspect to a crime scene.

These analyses support criminal investigations and help ensure justice is served.

Notable modern Greek scientists of modern times include Dimitrios Galanos, Georgios Papanikolaou (inventor of the Pap test), Nicholas Negroponte, Constantin Carathéodory, Manolis Andronikos, Michael Dertouzos, John Argyris, Panagiotis Kondylis, John Iliopoulos (2007 Dirac Prize for his contributions on the physics of the charm quark, a major contribution to the birth of the Standard Model, the modern theory of Elementary Particles), Joseph Sifakis (2007 Turing Award, the "Nobel Prize" of Computer Science), Christos Papadimitriou (2002 Knuth Prize, 2012 Gödel Prize), Mihalis Yannakakis (2005 Knuth Prize) and Dimitri Nanopoulos. def test_2():
    # Příklad vstupu, který je vytvořen pro testování
    text = "As of 2007, Greece had the eighth highest percentage of tertiary enrollment in the world (with the percentages for female students being higher than for male) while Greeks of the Diaspora are equally active in the field of education. Hundreds of thousands of Greek students attend western universities every year while the faculty lists of leading Western universities contain a striking number of Greek names. Notable modern Greek scientists of modern times include Dimitrios Galanos, Georgios Papanikolaou (inventor of the Pap test), Nicholas Negroponte, Constantin Carathéodory, Manolis Andronikos, Michael Dertouzos, John Argyris, Panagiotis Kondylis, John Iliopoulos (2007 Dirac Prize for his contributions on the physics of the charm quark, a major contribution to the birth of the Standard Model, the modern theory of Elementary Particles), Joseph Sifakis (2007 Turing Award, the \"Nobel Prize\" of Computer Science), Christos Papadimitriou (2002 Knuth Prize, 2012 Gödel Prize), Mihalis Yannakakis (2005 Knuth Prize) and Dimitri Nanopoulos."
    # Vyžadovaný výstup
    expected_result = True
    # Vstup do funkce
    prava_cisla = [2007, 2002, 2012, 2005]
    # Porovnání vstupu a výstupu
    assert any(year in prava_cisla for year in [int(word.split()[-1]) for word in text.split() if word.isdigit()]) == expected_result

instances.input: In the United States, the term "treaty" has a different, more restricted legal sense than exists in international law. United States law distinguishes what it calls treaties from executive agreement, congressional-executive agreements, and sole executive agreements. All four classes are equally treaties under international law; they are distinct only from the perspective of internal American law. The distinctions are primarily concerning their method of approval. Whereas treaties require advice and consent by two-thirds of the Senators present, sole executive agreements may be executed by the President acting alone. Some treaties grant the President the authority to fill in the gaps with executive agreements, rather than additional treaties or protocols. And finally, congressional-executive agreements require majority approval by both the House and the Senate, either before or after the treaty is signed by the President. Question: What  is  a treaty under international law?
instances.soft_output:
assistantlarge
Explanation: 'treaty' je v textu.
instances.inspect_output: A treaty under international law is a formal and legally binding agreement between sovereign states or international organizations. Treaties are governed by international law and can cover a wide range of subjects, including trade, peace, human rights, and environmental issues. They are typically negotiated and signed by representatives of the parties involved and require ratification according to the domestic laws of the states involved. Once ratified, treaties create obligations for the parties and are recognized as sources of international law.
instances.control_output: A treaty under international law is a formal and legally binding agreement between states or international organizations. It is governed by the principles of international law and can cover a wide range of subjects, including trade, peace, and human rights. Treaties are recognized as sources of international law and must be adhered to by the parties involved. They can take various forms, such as conventions, accords, or pacts, and are typically negotiated and signed by representatives of the states or organizations involved.

instances.input:
Prohibits the pay of such senior-level positions, after locality-based comparability payments, from exceeding the annual salary of the Vice President. Prohibits cash payments to the following Federal employees in a calendar year to the extent that, when added to basic pay, such amount would exceed the annual basic pay under level I of the Executive Schedule: (1) a member of the SES; (2) a member of the Federal Bureau of Investigation and Drug Enforcement Administration SES; (3) contract appeals board members; (4) certain senior-level positions; and (5) examiner-in-chief or designated examiner-in-chief in the Patent and Trademark Office, Department of Commerce.
Provides, with respect to such examiners and designated examiners: (1) an increase in the maximum pay rate to Level II of the Executive Schedule; and (2) authorized locality-based comparability payments.
instances.output: To amend title 5, United States Code, to alleviate the pay-compression problem affecting members of the Senior Executive Service and other senior-level Federal employees, and for other purposes.
instances.inspect_output:
The text outlines regulations regarding the compensation of certain senior-level federal employees. It specifies that:

1. The total pay for senior-level positions, including locality-based comparability payments, cannot exceed the annual salary of the Vice President.
2. Cash payments to specific federal employees are restricted so that, when combined with their basic pay, they do not surpass the annual basic pay of Level I of the Executive Schedule. The affected positions include:
   - Members of the Senior Executive Service (SES)
   - Members of the SES from the Federal Bureau of Investigation and Drug Enforcement Administration
   - Contract appeals board members
   - Certain senior-level positions
   - Examiners-in-chief or designated examiners-in-chief in the Patent and Trademark Office, Department of Commerce.
3. For the examiners and designated examiners mentioned, there are provisions for:
   - An increase in the maximum pay rate to Level II of the Executive Schedule.
   - Authorization for locality-based comparability payments.
instances.mapper_output: The text outlines restrictions on salary payments for senior-level federal positions, ensuring they do not exceed the Vice President's annual salary. It prohibits additional cash payments that, combined with basic pay, surpass the Executive Schedule's level I salary for specific roles, including SES members and certain examiners. Additionally, it allows for an increase in the maximum pay rate for designated examiners to level II of the Executive Schedule and permits locality-based comparability payments.
instances.fs_output: To amend title 5, United States Code, to address compensation limitations for senior-level Federal employees and to provide for adjustments in pay rates, and for other purposes.
instances.control_output: The policy restricts the compensation of certain senior-level federal positions, ensuring that their total pay, including locality-based comparability payments, does not surpass the annual salary of the Vice President. Additionally, it limits cash payments to specific federal employees, including members of the Senior Executive Service (SES), members of the SES in the FBI and DEA, contract appeals board members, certain senior-level positions, and designated examiners in the Patent and Trademark Office. The total compensation for these employees, when combined with their basic pay, cannot exceed the annual basic pay of Level I of the Executive Schedule. However, for examiners and designated examiners, the policy allows for an increase in their maximum pay rate to Level II of the Executive Schedule and permits locality-based comparability payments.

instances.input:
Measures to Encourage Results in Teaching Act of 1998 - Amends title II (Dwight D. Eisenhower Professional Development Program) of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to establish a new part D (State Incentives for Teacher Testing and Merit Pay). 
Directs the Secretary of Education to make an award to each State that:  (1) administers a test to each elementary school and secondary school teacher in the State, with respect to the subjects taught by the teacher, every three to five years; and (2) has an elementary school and secondary school teacher compensation system based on merit. 
Allows States to use Federal education funds for teacher testing and merit pay programs.

The Measures to Encourage Results in Teaching Act of 1998 amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 by adding a new part D, which focuses on State Incentives for Teacher Testing and Merit Pay. The Act requires the Secretary of Education to award funds to States that implement two key measures: 

1. Administering a test to every elementary and secondary school teacher every three to five years, tailored to the subjects they teach.
2. Establishing a teacher compensation system that rewards merit.

Additionally, the Act permits States to utilize Federal education funds to support these teacher testing and merit pay initiatives.

instances.input: School Anti-Violence Empowerment Act - Authorizes the Secretary of Education to award competitive grants to local educational agencies (LEAs) for: (1) crisis intervention programs, including school counselors; and (2) school safety programs for students, staff, and school facilities.Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to extend through FY 2006 the authorization of appropriations for certain grant programs for Community Policing and "Cops on the Beat" (COPS program). Eliminates provisions for decreasing Federal matching funds and for terminating grant program authority (thus making the authority permanent).Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to extend the authorization of appropriations for after-school and life skills programs for at-risk youth.Directs the Secretary to: (1) develop a model violence prevention program for LEAs; and (2) establish and maintain a national clearinghouse to provide LEAs with information on alternative violence prevention programs, and technical assistance to establish and operate them.
instances.output: To develop programs that enhance school safety for our children.
instances.inspect_output:
The School Anti-Violence Empowerment Act aims to enhance school safety and support for at-risk youth by authorizing the Secretary of Education to provide competitive grants to local educational agencies (LEAs). Key provisions include:

1. **Crisis Intervention Programs**: Funding for programs that include school counselors to help manage crises.
2. **School Safety Programs**: Grants to improve safety for students, staff, and school facilities.

Additionally, the Act amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to extend funding for Community Policing and the "Cops on the Beat" (COPS) program through FY 2006, while making the grant program authority permanent by eliminating provisions that would decrease federal matching funds or terminate the program.

Furthermore, it amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to continue funding for after-school and life skills programs aimed at at-risk youth.

The Secretary of Education is also directed to:
- Develop a model violence prevention program for LEAs.
- Establish a national clearinghouse to provide LEAs with information and technical assistance on alternative violence prevention programs.
instances.mapper_output: The School Anti-Violence Empowerment Act allows the Secretary of Education to grant funds to local educational agencies for crisis intervention and school safety programs. It extends funding for community policing initiatives and makes grant authority permanent. Additionally, it continues support for after-school programs for at-risk youth and mandates the development of a model violence prevention program, along with a national clearinghouse for resources and technical assistance for local agencies.
instances.fs_output: To authorize competitive grants for crisis intervention and school safety programs, to extend certain grant programs for Community Policing, and for other purposes.
instances.control_output:
The School Anti-Violence Empowerment Act aims to enhance school safety and support for at-risk youth by authorizing the Secretary of Education to provide competitive grants to local educational agencies (LEAs). These grants will fund crisis intervention programs, including the hiring of school counselors, and implement safety programs for students, staff, and school facilities. 

Additionally, the Act amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968, extending the authorization of appropriations for Community Policing and the "Cops on the Beat" (COPS) program through FY 2006. It also removes provisions that would decrease federal matching funds and terminates grant program authority, thereby making this authority permanent.

Furthermore, the Act amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to continue funding for after-school and life skills programs aimed at at-risk youth. The Secretary of Education is tasked with developing a model violence prevention program for LEAs and establishing a national clearinghouse to provide information and technical assistance on alternative violence prevention programs.
instances.soft2_output: To provide for the prevention of violence in schools, and for other purposes.

instances.input:
Adults Achieving the American Dream Act of 2007 - Amends the Adult Education and Family Literacy Act to reauthorize appropriations through FY2013 for adult education and literacy services, including integrated English literacy and civics education programs. Increases the maximum amounts that can be reserved from such funds for: (1) the National Institute for Literacy; and (2) national leadership activities. Reserves an amount for integrated English literacy and civics education programs.

Directs the Secretary of Education to award grants to states for integrated English literacy and civics education programs for immigrants admitted for lawful residence in the United States.

Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to reauthorize appropriations for Even Start Family Literacy Programs for FY2008 and each of the five succeeding fiscal years.

Amends the Education Sciences Reform Act of 2002 to require the establishment of a national research and development center that includes research in adult education.

Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a tax credit for employer-provided adult education and literacy programs.
instances.output: To amend the Adult Education and Family Literacy Act to establish integrated English literacy and civics education programs, and for other purposes.
instances.inspect_output:
The Adults Achieving the American Dream Act of 2007 is legislation that reauthorizes funding for adult education and literacy services through fiscal year 2013. Key provisions include:

1. **Reauthorization of Funds**: It amends the Adult Education and Family Literacy Act to continue appropriations for adult education and literacy services, with a focus on integrated English literacy and civics education programs.

2. **Increased Funding**: The act increases the maximum amounts that can be reserved for the National Institute for Literacy and for national leadership activities, ensuring more resources are allocated to these areas.

3. **Grants for States**: The Secretary of Education is directed to award grants to states specifically for integrated English literacy and civics education programs aimed at immigrants who are lawful residents of the United States.

4. **Even Start Family Literacy Programs**: It reauthorizes appropriations for Even Start Family Literacy Programs for fiscal year 2008 and the following five years, supporting family literacy initiatives.

5. **Research and Development Center**: The act requires the establishment of a national research and development center focused on adult education, as amended in the Education Sciences Reform Act of 2002.

6. **Tax Credit for Employers**: It amends the Internal Revenue Code to provide a tax credit for employers who offer adult education and literacy programs to their employees, incentivizing workplace education initiatives. 

Overall, the act aims to enhance adult education and literacy services, particularly for immigrants, and to support family literacy and research in the field.
instances.mapper_output: The Adults Achieving the American Dream Act of 2007 reauthorizes funding for adult education and literacy services through FY2013, enhancing support for integrated English literacy and civics education programs. It directs the Secretary of Education to grant funds to states for these programs aimed at immigrants. The Act also reauthorizes Even Start Family Literacy Programs and establishes a national research center for adult education. Additionally, it introduces a tax credit for employers providing adult education programs.
instances.fs_output: To amend the Adult Education and Family Literacy Act, the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965, and the Education Sciences Reform Act of 2002 to reauthorize appropriations for adult education and literacy services, and for other purposes.
instances.control_output:
The Adults Achieving the American Dream Act of 2007 reauthorizes funding for adult education and literacy services through FY2013, enhancing support for integrated English literacy and civics education programs. Key provisions include:

1. Increased funding limits for the National Institute for Literacy and national leadership activities.
2. Grants awarded by the Secretary of Education to states for integrated English literacy and civics education programs aimed at lawful immigrants.
3. Reauthorization of Even Start Family Literacy Programs for FY2008 and the following five years.
4. Establishment of a national research and development center focused on adult education as mandated by the Education Sciences Reform Act of 2002.
5. Introduction of a tax credit for employers who provide adult education and literacy programs.
instances.soft2_output: To amend the Adult Education and Family Literacy Act to reauthorize appropriations for adult education and literacy services, and for other purposes.

instances.input:
Drug-Free Workplace Program Reauthorization Act of 2004 - Amends the Small Business Act to extend through FY 2006 the authorization for the drug-free workplace demonstration program (program).

Authorizes the Administrator of the Small Business Administration to make an additional grant to, or enter into a cooperative agreement or contract with, any original grantee under such program for providing, in cooperation with one or more small business development centers, technical assistance to small businesses seeking to establish a program. Provides two-year periods for such grants. Makes drug-free communities coalitions eligible for such grant assistance.

Requires: (1) the Administrator to provide technical assistance to each intermediary receiving a grant regarding the most effective practices in establishing and carrying out such programs; (2) each intermediary to establish a system to collect and analyze information regarding the effectiveness of programs established with grant assistance; and (3) the Administrator to provide technical assistance and guidance to each intermediary with respect to the collection and analysis of such information.

Extends through FY 2006 the authority of small business development centers to provide information and assistance to small businesses with respect to establishing such programs.
instances.output: To amend the Small Business Act to reauthorize the Paul D. Coverdell Drug-Free Workplace Program, and for other purposes.

instances.inspect_output:
The Drug-Free Workplace Program Reauthorization Act of 2004 amends the Small Business Act to extend the authorization for the drug-free workplace demonstration program through fiscal year 2006. It allows the Administrator of the Small Business Administration to grant additional funding or enter into agreements with original grantees to provide technical assistance to small businesses aiming to establish drug-free workplace programs, in collaboration with small business development centers. 

Key provisions include:

1. Grants are available for two-year periods.
2. Drug-free communities coalitions are eligible for grant assistance.
3. The Administrator must provide technical assistance to intermediaries on effective practices for program implementation.
4. Intermediaries are required to collect and analyze data on the effectiveness of the programs funded by the grants.
5. The Administrator will also assist intermediaries in the collection and analysis of this information.
6. The authority for small business development centers to assist small businesses in establishing drug-free workplace programs is extended through FY 2006.
instances.mapper_output: The Drug-Free Workplace Program Reauthorization Act of 2004 extends the drug-free workplace demonstration program through FY 2006, allowing the Small Business Administration to grant additional funds to original grantees. It mandates technical assistance for small businesses and requires intermediaries to collect data on program effectiveness. The act also continues support for small business development centers in helping businesses establish drug-free workplace programs.
instances.fs_output: To amend the Small Business Act to extend the authorization for the drug-free workplace demonstration program through fiscal year 2006, and for other purposes.
instances.control_output:
The Drug-Free Workplace Program Reauthorization Act of 2004 amends the Small Business Act to extend the authorization for the drug-free workplace demonstration program through fiscal year 2006. It allows the Administrator of the Small Business Administration to grant additional funding or enter into cooperative agreements with original grantees to provide technical assistance to small businesses aiming to establish drug-free workplace programs, in collaboration with small business development centers. 

The Act stipulates that grants will have a two-year duration and makes drug-free communities coalitions eligible for assistance. It requires the Administrator to offer technical support to intermediaries receiving grants on effective practices for program implementation. Additionally, intermediaries must create a system to collect and analyze data on the effectiveness of the programs funded by these grants. The Administrator is also tasked with providing guidance on this data collection and analysis.

Furthermore, the authority of small business development centers to assist small businesses in establishing drug-free workplace programs is extended through fiscal year 2006.
instances.soft2_output: To amend the Small Business Act to reauthorize the drug-free workplace demonstration program, and for other purposes.

instances.input: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to: (1) allow through 2018 a new business-related tax credit for an increase in expenditures for qualified development expenses (defined as the sum of any in-house and contract development expenses incurred in the trade or business of a taxpayer), (2) increase to 20% the rate of the alternative simplified research tax credit, and (3) extend through 2018 the tax credit for increasing research activities.
instances.output: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to allow an increased credit for development and to extend and simplify the credit for increasing research.
instances.inspect_output:
The proposed amendments to the Internal Revenue Code include the following key provisions:

1. Introduction of a new business-related tax credit for an increase in expenditures on qualified development expenses, which encompasses both in-house and contract development costs incurred by taxpayers. This credit is applicable through the year 2018.

2. An increase in the rate of the alternative simplified research tax credit to 20%.

3. An extension of the existing tax credit for increasing research activities, also through the year 2018.
instances.mapper_output: The amendment to the Internal Revenue Code introduces a new business-related tax credit for increased qualified development expenses through 2018, raises the alternative simplified research tax credit rate to 20%, and extends the tax credit for increasing research activities until 2018.
instances.fs_output: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to provide for a new business-related tax credit for qualified development expenses, to increase the rate of the alternative simplified research tax credit, and to extend the tax credit for increasing research activities, and for other purposes.
instances.control_output:
The proposed amendment to the Internal Revenue Code includes the following key provisions: 

1. Introduction of a new business-related tax credit for increased expenditures on qualified development expenses, which encompasses both in-house and contract development costs incurred by taxpayers, effective through 2018.
2. An increase in the rate of the alternative simplified research tax credit to 20%.
3. An extension of the existing tax credit for increasing research activities, also through 2018.

instances.input:
Cybersecurity Education Enhancement Act of 2005 - Directs the Secretary of Homeland Security, acting through the Assistant Secretary of Cybersecurity, to establish, in conjunction with the National Science Foundation, a program to award grants to institutions of higher education for: (1) cybersecurity professional development programs; (2) associate degree programs in cybersecurity; and (3) the purchase of equipment to provide training in cybersecurity for either professional development or degree programs.

Requires the Director of the National Science Foundation to operate the program.

Amends the Homeland Security Act of 2002 to direct the Secretary to establish an e-security fellowship program to bring state, local, tribal, and private sector officials to participate in the work of the National Cybersecurity Division in order to become familar with Department of Homeland Security cybersecurity missions and capabilities.
instances.output: To authorize the Secretary of Homeland Security to establish a program to award grants to institutions of higher education for the establishment or expansion of cybersecurity professional development programs, and for other purposes.
instances.inspect_output:
The Cybersecurity Education Enhancement Act of 2005 mandates the Secretary of Homeland Security, through the Assistant Secretary of Cybersecurity, to collaborate with the National Science Foundation to create a grant program for higher education institutions. This program focuses on three key areas: 

1. Developing cybersecurity professional development programs.
2. Establishing associate degree programs in cybersecurity.
3. Funding the purchase of equipment necessary for training in cybersecurity, applicable to both professional development and degree programs.

Additionally, the Act requires the Director of the National Science Foundation to oversee the implementation of this program.

Furthermore, the Act amends the Homeland Security Act of 2002 to instruct the Secretary to create an e-security fellowship program. This program aims to engage state, local, tribal, and private sector officials in the operations of the National Cybersecurity Division, enhancing their understanding of the Department of Homeland Security's cybersecurity missions and capabilities.
instances.mapper_output: The Cybersecurity Education Enhancement Act of 2005 mandates the Secretary of Homeland Security to create a grant program, in collaboration with the National Science Foundation, for higher education institutions. This program focuses on cybersecurity professional development, associate degree programs, and equipment purchases for training. Additionally, it establishes an e-security fellowship program to engage officials from various sectors with the National Cybersecurity Division to enhance their understanding of cybersecurity missions and capabilities.
instances.fs_output: To enhance cybersecurity education and professional development through grants to higher education institutions, and for other purposes.
instances.control_output:
The Cybersecurity Education Enhancement Act of 2005 mandates the Secretary of Homeland Security, through the Assistant Secretary of Cybersecurity, to collaborate with the National Science Foundation to create a grant program for higher education institutions. This program focuses on three key areas: 

1. Developing cybersecurity professional development programs.
2. Establishing associate degree programs in cybersecurity.
3. Funding the acquisition of equipment necessary for training in cybersecurity, applicable to both professional development and degree programs.

Additionally, the Act requires the Director of the National Science Foundation to oversee the implementation of this program.

Furthermore, the Act amends the Homeland Security Act of 2002 to instruct the Secretary to create an e-security fellowship program. This program aims to engage state, local, tribal, and private sector officials in the operations of the National Cybersecurity Division, enhancing their understanding of the Department of Homeland Security's cybersecurity missions and capabilities.
instances.soft2_output: To provide for the establishment of a program to enhance cybersecurity education, and for other purposes.

instances.input: Question: How many years old was William FitzOxbern when the Priory Church was founded? Passage:It was founded around 1072 as a Benedictine priory by William FitzOsbern and his son Roger de Breteuil, 2nd Earl of Hereford. FitzOsbern had been granted the Lordship of Striguil by his second cousin King William in gratitude for his support in the Norman conquest of England, and was responsible for starting the building of a new castle overlooking the River Wye on the border with the kingdoms of Wales. At the same time he established a nearby monastic cell, so as to collect rent from the lands within Gwent which he had granted to his home Priory of Cormeilles in Normandy. By the early 12th century, the monastic establishment, on a ridge overlooking the river about 300 metres from the castle, had the status of an alien priory in its own right, though it probably never held more than about 12 monks. It superseded an earlier Augustinian priory located about 2 km away, which was dedicated to the Welsh saint Cynfarch (or St. Kingsmark), a disciple of St. Dyfrig.

instances.input: Question: What year was the song Shaft performed at the Academy Awards? Passage:The episode was written by Nell Scovell and directed by Wes Archer. In the episode, Bart and Lisa sneak into the sushi bar's karaoke room and sing the theme song to the 1971 film Shaft, "Theme from Shaft". The Fox network censors originally did not want the staff to use the song because they thought the lyrics were too obscene for television. In order to prove the censors wrong and show that it could appear on television, the staff dug up footage from an old Academy Awards ceremony at which the song was performed by Isaac Hayes. When the chef at the sushi bar finds out Homer has been poisoned, he yells at his apprentices in Japanese. The staff wanted the language they spoke to be actual Japanese, so they hired a Japanese actor who translated the lines for them. The episode introduced the character Akira, who has appeared many times later on the show. American actor George Takei provided the voice of Akira, although the character was voiced in later episodes by Hank Azaria. The episode featured many other guest appearances; Larry King as himself; Sab Shimono as the sushi bar chef; Joey Miyashima as Toshiro, the apprentice chef who slices up the fugu; and Diane Tanaka as hostess of the bar. King's role was first offered to American singer Bruce Springsteen, but he turned it down. According to showrunner Sam Simon, actor William Shatner also rejected the part.

instances.input: Question: Who was older, the Austrian at Ferrari or the number one driver at McLaren? Passage:He was replaced at Ferrari by Austrian Gerhard Berger for and he moved to McLaren as number two driver behind double and reigning World Champion Alain Prost. McLaren weren't as competitive in 1987 as they had been in 1984–1986, with Prost only adding three wins to his tally (and beating the record of 27 Grand Prix wins held by Jackie Stewart with his 28th win in Portugal) and failing to successfully defend his Drivers' Championship. Further podium finishes did follow for the Swede and Johansson finished sixth in the Drivers' Championship. Stefan Johansson's position at McLaren was considered by many as just a stop gap signing by team boss Ron Dennis who had failed to lure Ayrton Senna from Lotus due to him being under contract until the end of 1987 and always intended signing the Brazilian for . Johansson famously finished the 1987 German Grand Prix on three wheels having had a puncture on the last lap. He also finished second behind Prost in Belgium and added further podium finishes in Brazil, Spain and Japan. Despite 11 podiums in three seasons, Johansson was still winless and was not wanted by a top team (he had hoped to join Williams in 1988 as a replacement for the departing 1987 World Champion Nelson Piquet but Williams signed Riccardo Patrese instead). He did return to McLaren in a test-driver capacity in 1991, testing the Honda V12 engine at Suzuka in Japan.

instances.input: Question: How many years after the conclusion of World War I was Boris made a major general? Passage:He received his initial education in the so-called Palace Secondary School, which Ferdinand had created in 1908 solely for his sons. Later, Boris graduated from the Military School in Sofia, then took part in the Balkan Wars. During the First World War, he served as liaison officer of the General Staff of the Bulgarian Army on the Macedonian front. In 1916, he was promoted to colonel and attached again as liaison officer to Army Group Mackensen and the Bulgarian Third Army for the operations against Romania. Boris worked hard to smooth the sometimes difficult relations between Field Marshal Mackensen and Lieutenant General Stefan Toshev, the commander of the Third Army. Then the French repelled two Spanish attacks at Perpignan and Peyrestortes. Though the army was defeated again at Truillas and in other actions, the Spanish invaders withdrew to the Tech River in late 1793. Throughout the year the representatives on mission had enormous powers and used them to interfere with the military effort and to arrest officers that they deemed unpatriotic or unsuccessful. In 1794, the army's fortunes improved when Jacques François Dugommier took command. The army drove the Spanish army from France soil at Boulou and recaptured the Fort de Bellegarde and Collioure. After establishing itself on Spanish territory, the army won a decisive victory at the Battle of the Black Mountain in November during which Dugommier was killed. His replacement, Dominique Catherine de Pérignon soon captured the Sant Ferran fortress and the port of Roses. After these events the front became static and the last notable action was a Spanish victory at Bascara in June.

instances.input: Question: In what year did the Ford Mustang appear as a race car in the United States? Passage:Beechey began racing at the age of 22 in a Ford Customline V8. He came to prominence only a year later when he won the Olympic Touring Car Race at Albert Park, a support event at the Australian Grand Prix meeting which was held in conjunction with the 1956 Melbourne Olympic Games. As the expense of running this and two subsequent Customline V8s proved too prohibitive he reverted to a Holden 48-215 in 1959. After becoming part of David McKay’s Scuderia Veloce team he again returned to V8s, developing a Chevrolet Impala with which he won the New South Wales Touring Car Championship at Catalina Park. He progressed to a Ford Galaxie owned by Len Lukey and was then instrumental in forming the Neptune Racing Team in 1964. He raced a Holden EH S4 as part of that team alongside Jim McKeown’s Lotus Cortina and Peter Manton’s Morris Cooper S. He subsequently developed and raced a series of V8 powered Touring Cars with which he contested the Australian Touring Car Championship and other events. The first Ford Mustang to race in Australia was followed by a Chevrolet Chevy II Nova, a Chevrolet Camaro, a Holden Monaro GTS 327 and a Holden Monaro GTS 350.

instances.input: Question: Of the two educational facilities, which one has more land? Passage:State Street continues north through the Crescent neighborhood, past the site of the historic Crescent Elementary School, and enters Downtown Sandy. It intersects 10600 South, which becomes State Route 151 a short distance to the west, and passes a large retail area that includes big box stores and the South Towne Mall. The street climbs a small hill to pass over the Jordan and Salt Lake Canal, then passes a cemetery and a small amount of undeveloped greenfield land near the crossing of Dry Creek before reaching a larger hill into which the East Jordan Canal is carved. At the top of this hill, the street passes west of Jordan High School and the South Towne Expo Center before intersecting 9400 South (former State Route 209). A short distance north of that intersection, the East Jordan Canal passes back under the street on its way toward Holladay, and Rio Tinto Stadium fronts onto the street on the west side. Just before the intersection with 9000 South (modern State Route 209), State Street passes the site of a former smelter (which now has a medical center built on part of it). The street forms the western boundary of the Sandy Historic District from 9000 South to Pioneer Avenue (8530 South), and several historic buildings face onto it in this area. There is a traffic signal it the intersection with Sandy's Main Street at 8720 South. North of the historic district, State Street passes through some relatively lightly developed land near the city's border with Midvale, though there are some townhouse developments in progress in the area.

instances.input: Question: How old was Jeff Garcia during the 2007 NFL Season? Passage:The Buccaneers played their home opener against the 2006 NFC South champion New Orleans Saints. After scoring only 6 points the week before, the Buccaneers dominated the Saints, on offense and defense, at one point taking a 28–0 lead. Early in the first quarter, Barrett Ruud recovered a Deuce McAllister fumble, which set up a Tampa Bay scoring drive. Carnell Williams, who was back in the lineup after injuring his ribs the week before, capped off the drive with a one-yard touchdown run. Early in the second quarter, Joey Galloway scored on a 69-yard catch and run from quarterback Jeff Garcia. Inside the two-minute warning, Garcia and Galloway scored again, this time with a 24-yard touchdown, to take a 21–0 halftime lead. Halfway through the third quarter, Cato June intercepted a pass from Drew Brees. Two plays later, Garcia and Galloway connected for yet another big play, a 41-yard completion to the New Orleans 9-yard line. Williams capped off the drive with his second 1-yard touchdown. New Orleans' first score came with a 1-yard touchdown run by Mike Karney, aided by a 58-yard catch by Phillip Buchanon moments earlier. The Buccaneers improved to 1–1, into a tie for the NFC South lead. New Orleans fell to 0–2.

instances.input: Question: Which county had more people living in it the year Thomson returned to Seattle to work on the Diablo Dam, King or Pierce? Passage:From 1916 to 1922, Thomson served on the Seattle city council, while continuing to work as a civil engineer. After leaving the council, he continued working various places in the Pacific Northwest and Alaska. He consulted on Oregon's Rogue River Valley Irrigation Canal; built hydroelectric plants in Eugene, Oregon and surveyed plant sites in Southeastern Alaska; planned the water supply of Bellingham, Washington and consulted on the system for Wenatchee; briefly, in his seventies, he returned, temporarily, as Seattle city engineer in 1930 to finish the Diablo Dam on the Skagit River after the death of city engineer William D. Barkhuff; consulted to the Inter-County River Improvement Commission for King and Pierce Counties (the counties containing Seattle and Tacoma, respectively), and consulted on the construction of the Lake Washington Floating Bridge (now Lacey V. Murrow Memorial Bridge, carrying Interstate 90 across Lake Washington) and for the foundations of the Tacoma Narrows Bridge.

instances.input: Question: Who came second behind Diminuendo at The Oaks? Passage:In 1988 Melodist was ridden in all four of her races by Walter Swinburn. She did not run as a three-year-old until 22 May when she was sent to Italy for the Oaks d'Italia over 2400 metres on soft ground at the San Siro racecourse in Milan. The finish of the race was dominated by overseas challengers as Melodist won by one and a half lengths from her fellow British raider Asl (previously second in the Musidora Stakes) with the French-trained Summer Trip a neck away in third. On 9 July Melodist was one of nine runners to contest the Irish Oaks over one and a half miles at the Curragh and started at odds of 11/1. The 2/9 favourite for the race was Diminuendo, ridden by Steve Cauthen, who had won The Oaks by four lengths. Both Melodist and Diminuendo were owned by Sheikh Mohammed and based at Newmarket, but whereas Melodist was trained by Stoute, the favourite was trained by Henry Cecil at Warren Place. The other fancied runners were Dancing Goddess (second in the Irish 1000 Guineas), Miss Boniface (Ribblesdale Stakes) and Silver Lane (Prix de la Grotte). Melodist tracked the leaders before overtaking Miss Boniface before going to the front a furlong and a half from the finish. She faced a strong late challenge from Diminuendo and after a sustained struggle the two fillies crossed the line together with the judge declaring a dead heat.

instances.input: Question: How many citizens did the United Kingdom have the year Mire fled Somalia? Passage:Born in Mogadishu, Somalia in 1977, her father was a police criminal investigator who died of torture and lack of access to medical care due to political and clan-based violence when Mire was 12. After this traumatic experience, in 1991, she fled Somalia with her mother and siblings on a relative's lorry during the Somali Civil War. Mire and her identical twin, Sohur, emigrated to Sweden where an older sister lived and received asylum. The twins later moved to the United Kingdom to study. Mire studied Scandinavian pre-history and archaeozoology at Lund University in Sweden before receiving a BA degree in History of Art/Archaeology of Africa and Asia at SOAS, University of London in 2005, and subsequently an MA in African Archaeology in 2006 and PhD degree in Archaeology in 2009 at University College London.

hard_prompt:
In this task, you will use your knowledge about language (and common sense) to determine what element the marked number refers to. The numbers are marked with two underlines around them, like: _ number _. There are several possible answers, you'll need to choose the proper one. Carefully read the given text, pay special attention to the marked number, think about what (unwritten) information the marked number holds inside, choose the most adequate word(s) from the optional answers. If none of them seems right to you, there's also an option for other. If your answer is "REFERENCE", also write the reference entity, otherwise write the implicit option name. Options to choose from are:
REFERENCE: Some object which is being mentioned in the text before or after the target number. The reference answer has a higher priority than any other. If both Reference and another answer are possible, prioritize the Reference.
YEAR: Describing a calendric year
AGE: Describing someone's age
CURRENCY: Reference to some monetary value e.g dollar, euro etc.
PEOPLE: Describing a single/plural persons
TIME: Describing a time of the day. Usually you can add the word o'clock after those numbers.
OTHER: Some other option, which isn't listed here.

Peter: What , so she 'll have _ four _ ?
Andrew: Yes , but her agent gets one .

Herman Freitag: Your modesty does you credit . But sometimes the best - kept secrets leak out . You know what I mean ?
Col. Robert E. Hogan: I suppose you 're talking about the bombing mission I flew against your secret submarine base in Bremen ?

Herman Freitag: Yes . Mannheim , why do n't you see if my uniforms are back from the tailor ? And take Colonel Kink with you .
Col. Wilhelm Klink: Klink .
Herman Freitag: Whatever you say .

instances.input: Passage: Harvard has the largest university endowment in the world. As of September 2011[update], it had nearly regained the loss suffered during the 2008 recession. It was worth $32 billion in 2011, up from $28 billion in September 2010 and $26 billion in 2009. It suffered about 30% loss in 2008-09. In December 2008, Harvard announced that its endowment had lost 22% (approximately $8 billion) from July to October 2008, necessitating budget cuts. Later reports suggest the loss was actually more than double that figure, a reduction of nearly 50% of its endowment in the first four months alone. Forbes in March 2009 estimated the loss to be in the range of $12 billion. instances.input:
Katie:  She 's my sister , you 're just a slut !
Naomi: Oh , It takes one to know _ one _ , does n't it ?
Katie: Oh , go fuck yourself !
Naomi: You just needed to blab about JJ . You just loved doing it , did n't you , Katie ?
Katie: She deserved it !
Emily: What did I deserve , Katie ?
instances.soft_output:
1
Naomi: Oh, It takes one to know _ one, doesn't it? 
In this example, "one" is the answer because it's a noun, specifically a pronoun.

However, in the context of the conversation, "one" is used as a pronoun to refer to a person who is a slut, so the correct answer is "slut".

The other option "slut" is not a noun in this case, but a noun can be a type of noun (e.g. a proper noun) or a pronoun (e.g. "one"). In this case, "one" is a pronoun.

So, the final answer is "one". 
```python
def find_noun(sentence):
    # Tokenize the sentence
    tokens = sentence.split()
    
    # Check if the last token is a noun
    if tokens[-1].lower() in ["man", "woman", "people", "men", "women", "person", "people", "girl", "boy", "girls", "boys", "girlfriend", "boyfriend", "girls", "boys", "child", "children", "family", "families", "family", "families"]:
        return tokens[-1]
    
    # Check if the last token is a pronoun
    elif tokens[-1].lower() in ["i", "me", "my", "you", "your", "yours", "he", "him", "his", "she", "her", "hers", "it", "its", "we", "us", "our", "ours", "they", "them", "their", "theirs", "one", "ones"]:
        return tokens[-1]
    
    else:
        return None

sentence = "Oh, It takes one to know one, doesn't it?"
print(find_noun(sentence))
```

original question: Which apis have the protocol of [JSON]?
answer: hi5 api
answer type: API.
auxiliary information: JSON canonically pronounced /Ààd íe…™s…ôn/ JAY-s…ôn; sometimes JavaScript Object Notation, is an open standard format that uses human-readable text to transmit data objects consisting of attribute‚Äìvalue pairs. It is used primarily to transmit data between a server and web application, as an alternative to XML. Although originally derived from the JavaScript scripting language, JSON is a language-independent data format. Code for parsing and generating JSON data is readily available in many programming languages. The JSON format was originally specified by Douglas Crockford. It is currently described by two competing standards, RFC 7159 and ECMA-404. The ECMA standard is minimal, describing only the allowed grammar syntax, whereas the RFC also provides some semantic and security considerations. The official Internet media type for JSON is application/json. The JSON filename extension is .json.

instances.input:
original question: Find books whose genre is [New Wave science fiction]?
answer: in the valley of the kings
answer type: Book.
auxiliary information: The New Wave is a movement in science fiction produced in the 1960s and 1970s and characterized by a high degree of experimentation, both in form and in content, a "literary" or artistic sensibility, and a focus on "soft" as opposed to hard science. New Wave writers often saw themselves as part of the modernist tradition and sometimes mocked the traditions of pulp science fiction, which some of them regarded as stodgy, adolescent and poorly written.

instances.input:
NEW YORK (AP) -- Pfizer Inc. said Friday it is pulling its blood pressure drug Thelin off the market and stopping all clinical trials because the drug can cause fatal liver damage.
 Thelin is sold in the European Union  Canada  and Australia as an oral treatment for severe pulmonary arterial hypertension  or high blood pressure in the pulmonary artery. Pfizer said a review of data from clinical trials and post-marketing reports showed a new link to liver injury. It also consulted with experts about the link between Thelin and the deaths of two patients.
 Liver damage was a known side effect of Thelin and similar drugs  the company said. Pfizer said the withdrawal was voluntary and added that it has withdrawn its filing for marketing approval in the U.S.
 Since there are other treatment options  Pfizer said the benefits of Thelin don't outweigh the risks. It is stopping all studies of the oral drug  which Pfizer acquired in 2008 when it bought Encysive Pharmaceuticals Inc. Encysive had been trying to win marketing approval for Thelin since 2005  but the Food and Drug Administration said it was not effective enough. Other agencies only approved the drug for hypertension that was so debilitating that patients' physical activity was severely limited.
 The chemical name of Thelin is sitaxsentan.
instances.output: Citing liver damage Pfizer withdraws Thelin

Clinton also had some words for President Trump , particularly of his handling of Hurricane Maria and the devastation in Puerto Rico . 
Clinton , on her book tour for "What Happened ," called her memoir "a story of resilience ." 
Fallon also had female staff writers write thank you notes to Clinton . 
"Thank you , Miley , tonight 's show writers and all of the women and young girls out there who are smart , strong and deserving of every opportunity ," Clinton said . 
As for election night , Clinton said she was disappointed both that she lost and that President Trump won .

NBC News Fires  Matt Lauer  After Complaint Of 'Inappropriate Sexual Behavior'
NBC News says it has fired longtime Today host Matt Lauer following a complaint about "inappropriate sexual behavior in the workplace."
"On Monday night  we received a detailed complaint from a colleague about inappropriate sexual behavior in the workplace by Matt Lauer. It represented  after serious review  a clear violation of our company's standards. As a result  we've decided to terminate his employment. While it is the first complaint about his behavior in the over twenty years he's been at NBC News  we were also presented with reason to believe this may not have been an isolated incident."
 Lauer  joined Today as the news anchor in 1994 and became a co-anchor in 1997  according to NBC.  He  was the highest-paid person at NBC News  NPR's David Folkenflik notes.

Oklahoma’s crafty coach Lincoln Riley has a few tricks up his sleeve — not the least of which was a reverse  on third and two  with six seconds left in the first half  that had wide receiver CeeDee Lamb throwing a touchdown pass to quarterback  Baker Mayfield  to put Oklahoma up 31-17 heading into intermission.
There's so much offense in this game   Baker Mayfield  is CATCHING TOUCHDOWNS. pic.twitter.com/OyaiK8h8F8 — SportsCenter (@SportsCenter) January 1  2018
 Baker Mayfield : 1st career reception — ESPN Stats & Info (@ESPNStatsInfo) January 1  2018
With the play   Mayfield  becomes the first quarterback with a touchdown pass and touchdown reception in the Rose Bowl since Matt Leinart did it against Michigan to close out the 2003 season  per ESPN Stats and Info. Not a bad way to close out a dominant first half for the Heisman Trophy winner  who went 13-for-18 for 200 passing yards and a touchdown in the first half of the national semifinal.
So this is why Baker Mayfield was running routes before the game pic.twitter.com/gKoVX5lIXY — gifdsports (@gifdsports) January 1  2018
Baker Mayfield: 1st career reception — ESPN Stats & Info (@ESPNStatsInfo) January 1  2018
Oklahoma’s Baker Mayfield taunts ESPN with ‘pretenders’ sign at Rose Bowl
instances.output: College Football Playoff: Baker Mayfield and Oklahoma sprint out to a 17
instances.inspect_output: In a thrilling first half of the Rose Bowl, Oklahoma's coach Lincoln Riley showcased his innovative play-calling with a clever reverse play on third and two. With just six seconds remaining in the half, wide receiver CeeDee Lamb threw a touchdown pass to quarterback Baker Mayfield, extending Oklahoma's lead to 31-17. This play was particularly notable as it marked Mayfield's first career reception, making him the first quarterback since Matt Leinart in 2003 to achieve both a touchdown pass and a touchdown reception in the Rose Bowl. Mayfield had an impressive first half, completing 13 of 18 passes for 200 yards and a touchdown. His performance, along with the unique play, highlighted his versatility and skill on the field.

instances.input:
Dutch anti-Islam politician Geert Wilders went on trial Monday for alleged hate speech  even as his popularity and influence in the Netherlands are near all time highs.
 Prosecutors say Wilders incited hatred against Muslims with remarks comparing Islam to Naziism and by calling for a ban on the Quran. Wilders argues he has a right to freedom of speech and his remarks were within the bounds of the law.
 If convicted  he faces up to a year in prison. He could keep his seat in parliament.
 On his Twitter account  Wilders said the start of his trial was a "terrible day."
 "The freedom of expression of at least 1.5 million people is standing trial together with me " he wrote  referring to the voters that made his Freedom Party the third-largest in national elections in June.
 But his lawyer  Bram Moszkowicz  told presiding judge Jan Moors at the start of the trial that Wilders would not answer questions during the trial.
 lawyer  Bram Moszkowicz  told presiding judge Jan Moors at the start of the trial that Wilders would not answer questions during the trial.
 "My client will  at my advice  exercise his right to silence today  tomorrow and the other days " Moszkowicz said. Moors then adjourned the case to consider a request from Wilders to explain his decision not to speak.
 He swept into the Amsterdam District Court complex in a police convoy and waved to supporters as he walked into the courtroom at the start of a trial scheduled to last seven days.
 Mohamed Rabbae  chairman of the moderate National Moroccan Council  said outside the court that he hoped judges would force Wilders to issue an apology for his past remarks.
 "We are not for getting Mr. Wilders in prison. We are for correcting him " he said.
instances.output: Dutch politician on trial on hate speech charges

instances.inspect_output:
Dutch anti-Islam politician Geert Wilders went on trial Monday for alleged hate speech, despite his popularity and influence in the Netherlands being at an all-time high. Prosecutors accuse Wilders of inciting hatred against Muslims through remarks that compare Islam to Nazism and by advocating for a ban on the Quran. Wilders defends his statements as protected under freedom of speech and claims they are lawful.

If found guilty, he could face up to a year in prison, although he may retain his parliamentary seat. On Twitter, Wilders described the beginning of his trial as a "terrible day," stating, "The freedom of expression of at least 1.5 million people is standing trial together with me," referring to the voters who supported his Freedom Party, which became the third-largest party in the national elections in June.

Wilders' lawyer, Bram Moszkowicz, informed presiding judge Jan Moors that Wilders would not be answering questions during the trial. Moszkowicz stated, "My client will, at my advice, exercise his right to silence today, tomorrow, and the other days." The judge then adjourned the case to consider Wilders' request to explain his decision to remain silent.

Wilders arrived at the Amsterdam District Court in a police convoy, waving to supporters as he entered the courtroom for a trial expected to last seven days. Outside the court, Mohamed Rabbae, chairman of the moderate National Moroccan Council, expressed hope that the judges would compel Wilders to apologize for his previous comments, stating, "We are not for getting Mr. Wilders in prison. We are for correcting him."
instances.mapper_output: "Will Wilders answer questions during the trial?" Wilders will not answer questions during the trial.
instances.fs_output: Geert Wilders on trial for hate speech amid rising popularity.

Wilders defends his statements as a matter of freedom of speech, asserting that they are lawful.

On Twitter, Wilders described the beginning of his trial as a "terrible day," stating, "The freedom of expression of at least 1.5 million people is standing trial together with me," referring to the voters who supported his Freedom Party, which became the third-largest in the national elections in June.

Wilders' lawyer, Bram Moszkowicz, informed presiding judge Jan Moors that Wilders would not answer questions during the trial, stating, "My client will, at my advice, exercise his right to silence today, tomorrow, and the other days." The judge then adjourned the case to consider Wilders' request for an explanation regarding his decision to remain silent.

Wilders arrived at the Amsterdam District Court in a police convoy, waving to supporters as he entered the courtroom for the trial, which is expected to last seven days. Outside the court, Mohamed Rabbae, chairman of the moderate National Moroccan Council, expressed hope that the judges would compel Wilders to apologize for his previous remarks, stating, "We are not for getting Mr. Wilders in prison. We are for correcting him."

Is this the year  Scott Boras  fails to pull a rabbit out of a hat? Or three or four rabbits  to be precise?
 Scott Boras  is known for holding out to get the deals  he  wants  but it might not work this time. (Paul Sancya/AP)
Talking to scouts and executives on Wednesday  I heard  Boras â name a lot  as some are convinced  his  refusal to bend on  his  demands has created a stalemate for  his  top-of-the-market guys that has a trickle-down effect on other free agents.
