“I completely understand the value of native advertising,” Mr. McCambley said, “but there are a number of publishers who are allowing P.R. firms and advertising agencies direct access to their content management systems and allowing them to publish directly to the site. I think that is a huge mistake. 

“You are gambling with the contract you have with your readers,” Mr. McCambley said. “How do I know who made the content I am looking at and what the value of the information is?” 

Once inside, you find out that Mr. McCambley is not some crabby editorial type putting the old gimlet eye on the necessary but distasteful evil of advertising. All he ever wanted to do was make good content, and the banner ad is not Mr. McCambley’s only credential: The Wonderfactory has designed Web sites for National Geographic, Martha Stewart, Coca-Cola, and The Huffington Post. 

“We were proud of that ad,” said Mr. McCambley. “But everything starts out good until we end up making it bad.” 

Mr. McCambley is doing his own share of work on native advertising, but said, “it has to stand on its own as good journalism. Bad native advertising is destructive for the publishers that host it.”

Melanie Oudin and Francesca Schiavone also know how quickly perceptions can change. Oudin struggled with higher expectations since her crowd-pleasing run to the U.S. Open quarterfinals last year. So did Schiavone after her breakthrough French Open title in June.
 Oudin  18  needed just 56 minutes to beat Olga Savchuk of Ukraine 6-3  6-0. Schiavone  the Italian who won her first Grand Slam weeks before her 30th birthday  dispatched Ayumi Morita of Japan 6-1  6-0 in 58 minutes.
 If anything  Schiavone seems to be having fun in the spotlight. Asked why she's a fan favorite  she playfully replied  "I attract them because I'm beautiful."
 Schiavone acknowledged that maybe she's a bit more motivated at a Grand Slam than at other tournaments. Schiavone  seeded No. 6  had been just 3-6 since winning at Roland Garros. She lost in the first round at Wimbledon and dropped her opening match at three other tournaments.
 She was pleased that her first-round match was in the grandstand -- a year ago  she was relegated to an outer court.
 "I like to do it  because adrenaline is coming up and I enjoy much more than play in faraway court " she said with a laugh. "Maybe because I am 30 years old and now I want to enjoy with people."

instances.inspect_output:
Melanie Oudin and Francesca Schiavone have experienced the shifting nature of public perception in tennis. Oudin, at just 18, faced heightened expectations following her impressive run to the U.S. Open quarterfinals last year. Similarly, Schiavone dealt with the pressure that came after her historic French Open victory in June, which marked her first Grand Slam title just before her 30th birthday.

In their recent matches, Oudin quickly defeated Olga Savchuk from Ukraine with a score of 6-3, 6-0 in only 56 minutes. Schiavone, the Italian star, also showcased her skills by dispatching Ayumi Morita of Japan with a commanding 6-1, 6-0 victory in 58 minutes.

Schiavone appears to be relishing her time in the spotlight. When asked about her popularity among fans, she humorously stated, "I attract them because I'm beautiful." She admitted that her motivation tends to be higher during Grand Slam events compared to other tournaments. Despite her recent struggles, with a record of just 3-6 since her triumph at Roland Garros, Schiavone expressed her excitement about playing in the grandstand, a significant upgrade from being placed on an outer court the previous year.

"I like to do it because adrenaline is coming up and I enjoy much more than play in faraway court," she said with a laugh. "Maybe because I am 30 years old and now I want to enjoy with people."

Melanie Oudin and Francesca Schiavone have experienced the rapid shifts in public perception. Oudin faced heightened expectations following her impressive run to the U.S. Open quarterfinals last year, while Schiavone dealt with similar pressures after her historic French Open victory in June.

In their first-round matches, Oudin, 18, quickly defeated Olga Savchuk of Ukraine with a score of 6-3, 6-0 in just 56 minutes. Schiavone, the Italian who claimed her first Grand Slam title shortly before turning 30, also had a commanding performance, winning against Ayumi Morita of Japan 6-1, 6-0 in 58 minutes.

Schiavone appears to be relishing her time in the spotlight. When asked about her popularity among fans, she humorously stated, "I attract them because I'm beautiful." She admitted that she feels a heightened motivation during Grand Slam events compared to other tournaments. Despite being seeded No. 6, Schiavone had a challenging period post-Roland Garros, with a record of just 3-6, including a first-round exit at Wimbledon and losses in her opening matches at three other tournaments.

She expressed her satisfaction with playing her first-round match in the grandstand, contrasting it with her experience the previous year when she was assigned to an outer court. "I like to do it because adrenaline is coming up and I enjoy much more than play in faraway court," she said with a laugh. "Maybe because I am 30 years old and now I want to enjoy with people."

1. **Your Cousin's Feelings**: How does your cousin feel about the situation? If he is upset about losing his Xbox, he might not appreciate the gesture as much as you hope.

Overall, it seems like you have good intentions, so you may not be the AITA in this scenario, but it ultimately depends on how your cousin perceives the situation.

It sounds like you're in a situation where you're navigating your own happiness and the expectations of your parents. It's important to prioritize your own feelings and choices in a relationship, especially if you feel fulfilled and happy. 

Your parents may have strong opinions based on their values or concerns for your well-being, but ultimately, you are an adult capable of making your own decisions. It's understandable that they might be worried about the age difference or the dynamics of your relationship, but that doesn't mean you are in the wrong for pursuing what makes you happy.

You are not the asshole for choosing to date someone who makes you feel good about yourself. However, it might be helpful to have an open conversation with your parents about your relationship and your feelings, to help them understand your perspective. Communication can sometimes ease tensions and help them see that you are making choices that are right for you.

It sounds like you found yourself in a tricky situation. While your intention may not have been malicious, taking over someone else's account without reporting it can be problematic. The account likely belongs to someone else, and by changing the information, you could be infringing on their rights or privacy. 

In this case, it would be best to report the situation to Target's customer service. They can help you create a new account and investigate the existing one. It's understandable to want to resolve the issue quickly, but reporting it would have been the more appropriate action. 

So, while you may not be an "asshole" in the traditional sense, it would have been better to handle it differently. It's a learning experience, and moving forward, reporting such issues is the right approach.

instances.input:
Me [M19] and my girlfriend [F19] have this thing we do where we pick out different move series that we watch in case we have nothing planned for the days that we hang out. Harry Potter is our 3rd movie series that we decided to watch because I had never seen the Harry Potter movies and she had seen them multiple times because it’s one of her favorites. Before that I chose the Lord of the Rings series because she hadn’t seen that one and it’s one of my favorites . Sometimes we have to cut our movie time short because one of us had to go back to our house or it was time for bed (if one of us are sleeping over) well the other day we had to cut “The order of the Phoenix” short, about 45 minutes before it ended because she had to go back to her house and we wouldn’t see each other for a couple days later. Well the day after our movie time I was bored because it was my day off (were college students and I work a part time job) and I almost never have a lot of spare time. I decided to watch the last 45 minutes of the movie and I have to say it was pretty good. When I told her I finished the movie she seemed pretty upset, we didn’t fight or anything and there was no yelling or tears involved but she still looked pretty upset. And she had done  the same thing before when we watched the Lord of the Rings series on the Two Towers movie so I didn’t think it was a big deal and didn’t care that she did it. I told her this but I still feel bad about the whole thing. Am I the ass?

TL:DR: me and my gf watch different movie series with each other and I finished a movie without her.

instances.input:
Some backstory:

* I am my mom's only child (my dad has three other daughters he does not talk to much),
* I am stationed overseas with my husband for one more year and then we are stationed back into the states,
* it has been a year and a half since I have seen them (the last time I saw them, I flew from overseas to the states).

So recently, my parents have been very adamant about my coming to visit them this year. However, I am getting very tired of it and want to be straightforward with them. It isn't about the cost of the ticket.

It's about the time it takes for me to get home, seeing as they live in a small town far from a main airport. It's also about the fact that they would push for me to stay for two weeks or more. This goes against my current schedule in college and my gym time (though that is flexible rather than college). When they do bring up my flying to visit them, I bring up these issues and they say it will be fine, but that I really should stay 2+ weeks.

Would I be the asshole if I told my parents I don't want to visit them until next year, when I am back in the states? Is there a better way to word this? Do I have to be very straightforward with them?
instances.output: telling my parents I don't want to visit them until next year
instances.soft_output: #not visiting my parents for another year, even though they want me to visit them this year. I have a good reason for not wanting to visit them this year, but they are not listening to me. They want me to visit them for 2 weeks or more, but I don't have the time for that. They will be very upset when I tell them this. I don't want to hurt their feelings, but I also don't want to visit them this year. I am looking for a way to word this without hurting their feelings, or is that even possible? I am not the asshole here, I am the one with the reason. I am the one who has to deal with the consequences of visiting them for 2 weeks or more. I am the one who has to deal with the stress of visiting them, and the guilt of not visiting them for so long. They are the ones who are being unreasonable. They are the ones who are not listening to me. They are the ones who are pushing for me to visit them, even though they know I don't want to. They are the ones who are being the asshole. I am just trying to be honest with them, and I am the asshole for being honest with them? This is ridiculous. I am not the asshole. They are the asshole. I am just trying to be honest with them. I am not the asshole. They are the asshole. I am just trying to be honest with them. I am not the asshole. They are the asshole. I am just trying to be honest with them. I am not the asshole. They are the asshole. I am just trying to be honest with them. I am not the asshole. They are the asshole. I am just trying to be honest with them. I am not the asshole. They are the asshole. I am just trying to be honest with them. I am not the asshole. They are the asshole. I am just trying to be honest with them. I am not the asshole. They are the asshole. I am just trying to be honest with them. I am not the asshole. They are the asshole. I am just trying to be honest with them. I am not the asshole. They are the asshole. I am just trying to be honest with them. I am not
instances.inspect_output:
It's understandable that you're feeling overwhelmed by your parents' insistence on a visit, especially given your current commitments and the logistics involved. It's important to communicate your feelings honestly while also being respectful of their wishes. 

You might consider saying something like this:

"Mom and Dad, I really appreciate how much you want to see me, and I miss you too. However, with my current schedule in college and the logistics of traveling to see you, I don't think I can make a visit work this year. I want to make sure that when I do come, I can fully enjoy the time with you without feeling rushed or stressed. I think it would be best for me to plan a visit next year when I'm back in the states and can spend quality time with you without the constraints I have right now."

This way, you're being straightforward about your situation while also expressing your desire to see them in the future. It shows that you value your relationship with them and want to make the visit meaningful.
instances.mapper_output: How can I communicate to my parents that I prefer not to visit them this year and would rather wait until next year when I am back in the states?
instances.fs_output: not wanting to visit my parents until next year due to time constraints and their insistence on a long stay
instances.control_output:
It's understandable that you're feeling overwhelmed by your parents' insistence on a visit, especially given your current commitments and the logistics involved. It's important to communicate your feelings honestly while also being respectful. Here’s a way to approach the conversation:

1. **Acknowledge Their Feelings**: Start by recognizing that you understand they want to see you and that it’s been a long time since your last visit.

2. **Express Your Situation**: Clearly explain your current commitments, such as your college schedule and the challenges of traveling to their location. You might say something like, "I really appreciate how much you want to see me, and I miss you both too. However, with my college schedule and the travel logistics, I’m finding it difficult to make a visit work right now."

3. **Set Boundaries**: Be honest about your needs. You could say, "I think it would be best for me to plan a visit when I’m back in the states next year. That way, I can spend quality time with you without feeling rushed or stressed."

4. **Offer Alternatives**: Suggest other ways to stay connected in the meantime, such as regular video calls or planning a visit when you’re back in the states.

5. **Be Firm but Kind**: It’s okay to be straightforward about your decision. You can say, "I hope you understand that I need to prioritize my current responsibilities, and I’m looking forward to visiting when I can fully enjoy the time with you."

By approaching the conversation this way, you can communicate your feelings without coming off as dismissive or ungrateful. It’s important to prioritize your well-being while also being considerate of their feelings.
instances.soft2_output: not wanting to visit my parents

instances.input:
So I just left a grocery store at the same time as another group of people, and our cars were right next to eachother. They were standing inbetween my car and theirs talking with the cart, and i said excuse me to them so that I could open my door to get it, but they didn't hear me, so I opened it as much as I could without hitting them and squeezed in.

I put my reverse lights on to back up and they still were standing right next to my car, and the cart was over the line a bit and I didn't want to risk hitting it or them. So, I opened the door to ask if they could move their cart up a bit and said thanks when they did, and the lady yelled at me "Jesus. We are just trying to pack our car." I said to them I was just trying to reverse and not hit them, but they continued to point at me and around after I closed the door. I think maybe they took it as me being angry that they were next to my car instead of me trying to hit them, but am I the asshole for asking them to move?

instances.input:
I work at a university in Ontario, Canada and there are hundreds of staff in this school. So I don't know the guy in question, have no relationship with him. Frequently he takes his lunch break in the staff room at the same time as me (which is pretty large, could seat around 20-30 people). 

Very rarely does anyone chat– People usually come here just to eat lunch or take a break. Additionally, there's usually only 5 people in here at any one time so it's very quiet. 

This guy eats with his mouth open, and it's the sound that really bugs me. It's all you can hear. If I told him to stop chewing and eating his food like that, would I be the asshole?
instances.output: asking a co-worker to stop chewing with their mouth open
instances.soft_output: lastly telling someone to stop eating with their mouth open in the staff room at work, which is a public area, but still a private space where people go to relax and eat lunch. I don't know the guy and never will. I just don't want to have to hear it anymore. I've tried to avoid the staff room during his lunch break, but I have to go there for meetings and sometimes I'm stuck in the staff room with him for a bit. instances.input:
Text: so, i worked the morning shift starting at 6:30 am at a gas/convenience 
normally, i set my alarm one hour before i need to be awake and snooze my way through: this is how i boot my brain up. by the time i snooze ten times, i'm pretty self-aware.


except i decided to sleep until i was on in half-an-hour, 15 minutes to get ready and 15 to get there.


and within ten minutes of starting, i make a terrible, terrible mistake.
running the pos, i mistake bal (balance due) as chg (change due).
so, a customer pays $20 out of a $26.12 purchase. i put in $20 from the customer. i thought it was odd that the register wouldn't print a receipt, but the customer didn't want it. so i overrode the register and handed them $6.12.


fast-forward ten minutes, i realize that my till will be short $12.24. because the next customer that pays cash makes me think about bal and chg. i didn't want to be short anything, so my first purchase with payday money is covering  my mistake right back to my employer.

 
it's not even that much, go back and read it. i typed it on mobile too.
instances.output: TIFU by not waking up early enough

tl; dr: bumped into a glass window with almost everyone looking, developed a big red bruise on my forehead that people will ask about on monday.

instances.input: Passage: 'Most archaeologists believe that Machu Picchu was constructed as an estate for the Inca emperor Pachacuti (1438 -- 1472). Often mistakenly referred to as the 'Lost City of the Incas' (a title more accurately applied to Vilcabamba), it is the most familiar icon of Inca civilization. The Incas built the estate around 1450 but abandoned it a century later at the time of the Spanish Conquest. Although known locally, it was not known to the Spanish during the colonial period and remained unknown to the outside world until American historian Hiram Bingham brought it to international attention in 1911.'. Question: 'where is earwax located and produced in your ear why is it important'.
instances.inspect_output:
Earwax, also known as cerumen, is located in the ear canal, which is the passage that leads from the outer ear to the eardrum. It is produced by glands in the skin of the ear canal. 

Earwax serves several important functions: 

1. **Protection**: It helps to trap dust, debris, and microorganisms, preventing them from reaching the eardrum and potentially causing infections.

2. **Lubrication**: Earwax keeps the skin of the ear canal moist, preventing dryness and itching.

3. **Antimicrobial properties**: It has natural antibacterial and antifungal properties, which help to protect the ear from infections.

Overall, earwax plays a crucial role in maintaining ear health.

2. **Lubrication**: It keeps the skin of the ear canal moist, preventing dryness and itching.
3. **Antimicrobial properties**: Earwax has natural antibacterial and antifungal properties, which help to protect the ear from infections.

Overall, earwax is essential for maintaining ear health and protecting the auditory system.

def is_book_related(question, book_passages):
    """
    Check if a question is related to any of the given book passages.

    Args:
        question (str): The question to be checked.
        book_passages (dict): A dictionary where keys are book titles and values are book passages.

    Returns:

    return False

instances.inspect_output:
**Garlic Bread with Tomato and Mozzarella**

**Ingredients:**
- French bread (mini loaves)
- 1 1/2 sticks butter, room temperature
- 5 to 6 cloves garlic, finely minced
- Tomato, thinly sliced
- Mozzarella cheese, sliced thin
- Oregano

**Directions:**
1. Mix butter and minced garlic together in a bowl.
2. Place the garlic butter mixture in the fridge to chill, then bring it back to room temperature before using.
3. This garlic butter can be made a day in advance for convenience.
4. Slice the French bread lengthwise.
5. Spread the garlic butter evenly on the cut sides of the bread.
6. Arrange the thinly sliced tomatoes across the buttered bread.
7. Cover the tomatoes with slices of mozzarella cheese.
8. Sprinkle oregano on top of the cheese.
9. Broil in the oven at 400°F until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
10. Cut into slices and serve hot. Enjoy!
instances.mapper_output: This recipe involves preparing garlic bread with tomatoes and mozzarella cheese.

instances.inspect_output:
**Recipe: Fruit and Cream Cheese Ball**

**Ingredients:**
- 1 - 20 oz. can crushed pineapple, drained well
- 1 - 16 oz. can sliced peaches, drained and chopped small
- 1 small bottle cherries, drained and sliced
- 1 small box instant vanilla pudding
- 2 - 8 oz. packages cream cheese
- 1 pkg. sliced almonds

**Directions:**
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the crushed pineapple, sliced peaches, cherries, instant vanilla pudding, and cream cheese.
2. Mix all the ingredients together until well combined.
3. Form the mixture into a large ball shape.
4. Cover the ball with sliced almonds, pressing them gently into the surface.
5. Wrap the ball in plastic wrap and place it in the refrigerator overnight to chill.
6. Serve the fruit and cream cheese ball with Ritz or Townhouse crackers. Enjoy!
instances.mapper_output: Mix all ingredients together, except almonds, and form into a large ball. Cover with almonds and place in refrigerator over night. Serve with Ritz or Townhouse crackers.
instances.control_output:
**Pineapple Peach Cream Cheese Ball**

**Ingredients:**
- 1 - 20 oz. can crushed pineapple, drained well
- 1 - 16 oz. can sliced peaches, drained and chopped small
- 1 small bottle cherries, drained and sliced
- 1 small box instant vanilla pudding
- 2 - 8 oz. packages cream cheese
- 1 pkg. sliced almonds

**Directions:**
1. In a large mixing bowl, combine the crushed pineapple, chopped peaches, sliced cherries, instant vanilla pudding, and cream cheese. Mix well until all ingredients are thoroughly combined.
2. Form the mixture into a large ball shape.
3. Roll the ball in the sliced almonds, ensuring it is well coated.
4. Wrap the ball in plastic wrap and place it in the refrigerator overnight to chill and firm up.
5. Serve the cream cheese ball with Ritz or Townhouse crackers. Enjoy!

instances.inspect_output:
**Sweet Potato Chocolate Bundt Cake**

**Ingredients:**
- 1 1/4 pounds sweet potatoes, golden, peeled and diced
- 5 eggs
- 2 1/4 cups granulated sugar
- 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
- 2 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 cup canola oil
- 7 ounces dark chocolate, coarsely chopped
- Powdered sugar for dusting

**Directions:**
1. Bring 1/2 cup of water to a boil in a pan. Add the diced sweet potatoes, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes. Drain the potatoes, return them to the pan, and mash. Set aside to cool.
2. Preheat the oven to 325°F (165°C). Grease and flour a 9-inch bundt pan.
3. In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, granulated sugar, vanilla extract, and a pinch of salt until thick.
4. Add the cooled mashed sweet potatoes to the egg mixture and mix well.
5. Fold in the all-purpose flour and baking powder until just combined.
6. Add the canola oil and chopped dark chocolate, mixing until evenly distributed.
7. Transfer the batter to the prepared bundt pan and bake for 60-70 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
8. Remove from the oven and let cool in the pan for 15 minutes. Then, carefully remove the cake from the pan and let it cool completely on a wire rack.
9. Dust with powdered sugar before serving. Enjoy!
instances.mapper_output: This recipe involves making a sweet potato chocolate cake.
instances.control_output:
**Sweet Potato Chocolate Bundt Cake**

**Ingredients:**
- 1 1/4 pounds sweet potatoes, golden, peeled and diced
- 5 eggs
- 2 1/4 cups granulated sugar
- 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
- 2 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
- 3 teaspoons baking powder
- 1 cup canola oil
- 7 ounces dark chocolate, coarsely chopped
- Powdered sugar for dusting

**Directions:**
1. Bring 1/2 cup of water to a boil. Add the diced sweet potatoes, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes. Drain the potatoes, return them to the pan, and mash. Set aside to cool.
2. Preheat the oven to 325°F (165°C). Grease and flour a 9-inch bundt pan.
3. In a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, granulated sugar, vanilla extract, and a pinch of salt until thick.
4. Add the cooled mashed sweet potatoes to the egg mixture and mix well.
5. Fold in the all-purpose flour and baking powder until just combined.
6. Add the canola oil and chopped dark chocolate, mixing until evenly distributed.
7. Transfer the batter to the prepared bundt pan and bake for 60-70 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
8. Remove from the oven and let cool in the pan for 15 minutes. Then, carefully remove the cake from the pan and let it cool completely on a wire rack.
9. Dust with powdered sugar before serving. Enjoy!

**Ingredients:**
- 1-1/2 lbs (0.7 kg) beef tips
- 5 small potatoes
- 3 medium carrots
- 1/2 can peas
- 1/2 can corn
- 1 can cream of tomato soup
- 1 can cream of celery soup
- 1 can French onion soup

**Directions:**
1. Place beef tips in the bottom of a baking pan.
2. Cut up potatoes and place them on top of the beef tips.
3. Slice carrots and place them on top of the potatoes.
4. Pour water off of peas and corn and place them on top of the carrots.
5. Spread tomato soup on top.
6. Next, spread celery soup on the tomato soup.
7. Pour French onion soup over the entire ingredients.
8. DO NOT STIR!!
9. Cover with Reynold's Wrap and place in a 350°F (175°C) oven.
10. Bake for 3 1/2 to 4 hours.
11. When done, take out of the oven and stir.

instances.inspect_output:
**Recipe: Graham Cracker Toffee Bars**

**Ingredients:**
- 1 package (10 to 12 pieces) Graham Crackers
- 2 sticks Butter
- 1 cup Brown Sugar
- 1 cup Chocolate Chips
- 1/2 cup Slivered or Sliced Almonds

**Directions:**
1. Line an 11x15 inch cookie sheet with whole graham crackers, filling the entire space as needed.
2. In a pan, mix the butter and brown sugar and bring to a boil. Stir continuously for 5 minutes while boiling on low heat.
3. Spread the butter and sugar mixture evenly over the graham crackers. Bake in a preheated oven at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 7 minutes.
4. Once out of the oven, sprinkle the chocolate chips and slivered almonds on top. Allow to cool in the fridge for 10-20 minutes.
5. Break into pieces to serve.

**Alternative Method:**
After sprinkling the chocolate chips, let them melt for a minute, then spread the melted chocolate over the caramel layer before adding the nuts. This method is recommended for a smoother finish, as shown in the photographed version.
instances.mapper_output: Line an 11x15 inch cookie sheet with whole crackers. It will fill up pretty much the whole space. Use as many whole crackers as you need to fill the space.

**Recipe: Graham Cracker Treats**

- 1 package (10 to 12 pieces) Graham Crackers
- 2 sticks Butter
- 1 cup Brown Sugar
- 1 cup Chocolate Chips
- 1/2 cup Slivered or Sliced Almonds

**Directions:**
1. Line an 11x15 inch cookie sheet with whole graham crackers, filling the entire space as needed.
2. In a pan, mix the butter and brown sugar and bring to a boil. Stir for 5 minutes while boiling on low heat.
3. Spread the butter and sugar mixture over the lined crackers and bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 7 minutes.
4. Once out of the oven, sprinkle with chocolate chips and slivered almonds. Cool in the fridge for 10-20 minutes.
5. Break into pieces to serve.

**Alternative Method:**
- After sprinkling with chocolate chips, let the chocolate melt for a minute, then spread it out over the caramel before adding the nuts. This method is used in the photographed version.

instances.inspect_output:
**Recipe: Pinto Bean and Vegetable Stew**

**Ingredients:**
- 1 cup sliced carrots
- 1/2 head fresh broccoli
- 1 lb. bag pinto beans
- 1 can whole tomatoes
- 1 clove garlic
- 1 tsp. black pepper (optional)
- 2 Tbsp. chili powder
- 1 tsp. cumin
- 1 tsp. salt (to taste)
- 1 medium onion
- 1 bell pepper
- Vegetable oil (for sautéing)

**Directions:**
1. Soak the pinto beans for about 2 hours.
2. Cook the soaked beans in a medium-sized pot until tender.
3. While the beans are cooking, chop all the vegetables (onion, bell pepper, garlic, carrots, and broccoli) and keep them separate.
4. In a skillet, sauté the onion, bell pepper, garlic, and carrots in vegetable oil until they are softened.
5. Chop the canned tomatoes a little and add them to the cooked beans along with the sautéed vegetables (carrots, onion, bell pepper, and garlic).
6. Add the chili powder, black pepper (if using), cumin, and salt to the mixture. Let it simmer for 20 minutes.
7. Finally, add the broccoli to the pot and cook for a few more minutes until the broccoli is tender.
8. Serve the stew with cornbread or spicy hot cheese cornbread and rice. Enjoy!
instances.mapper_output: The recipe includes ingredients such as sliced carrots, fresh broccoli, pinto beans, whole tomatoes, garlic, black pepper, chili powder, cumin, salt, onion, and bell pepper, and the directions involve soaking and cooking the beans, sautéing the vegetables, and simmering everything together.

instances.input: ingredients:  '2 c. cooked rice', '1 c. chopped onions', '10 oz. chopped broccoli, partially thawed', '1 can cream of mushroom soup', '1/4 c. milk', '8 oz. jar jalapeno or plain Cheez Whiz', '1 lb. peeled crawfish tails', '2 Tbsp. margarine', '1 c. chopped bell pepper',<sep> directions: 'In a 5-quart casserole, combine margarine, onions and bell pepper.', 'Microwave on High for 5 minutes.', 'Add broccoli and microwave on High an additional 5 minutes.', 'Add remaining ingredients; season to taste and microwave on High for 10 minutes, stirring once.', 'Serve with salad and hot French bread.', 'Serves 8.'
instances.inspect_output:
**Crawfish and Broccoli Rice Casserole**

**Ingredients:**
- 2 cups cooked rice
- 1 cup chopped onions
- 10 oz. chopped broccoli, partially thawed
- 1 can cream of mushroom soup
- 1/4 cup milk
- 8 oz. jar jalapeno or plain Cheez Whiz
- 1 lb. peeled crawfish tails
- 2 tablespoons margarine
- 1 cup chopped bell pepper

**Directions:**
1. In a 5-quart casserole, combine margarine, onions, and bell pepper.
2. Microwave on High for 5 minutes.
3. Add broccoli and microwave on High for an additional 5 minutes.
4. Add remaining ingredients; season to taste and microwave on High for 10 minutes, stirring once.
5. Serve with salad and hot French bread.

**Serves:** 8
instances.mapper_output: This recipe combines cooked rice, chopped onions, broccoli, cream of mushroom soup, milk, Cheez Whiz, crawfish tails, margarine, and bell pepper, and is prepared by microwaving the ingredients in a casserole.

4. Add remaining ingredients; season to taste and microwave on High for 10 minutes, stirring once.
5. Serve with salad and hot French bread.

instances.inspect_output:
**Shrimp and Gemelli Pasta with Fennel**

**Ingredients:**
- 2 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil
- 1/2 cup finely chopped onion
- 1/2 cup finely chopped fresh fennel bulb
- 1/2 cup finely chopped red bell pepper
- 1 teaspoon lightly crushed fennel seeds
- 1 cup crushed canned tomatoes
- 1/2 pound shelled medium shrimp
- 1/4 cup heavy cream
- Salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
- 1/2 pound gemelli pasta
- 1/2 tablespoon finely chopped Italian parsley

**Directions:**
1. Heat the olive oil in a heavy skillet over medium heat.
2. Add the chopped onion, fennel, and red bell pepper. Sauté until all the vegetables are tender.
3. Stir in the crushed fennel seeds and the canned tomatoes. Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for about 15 minutes.
4. Add the shrimp to the skillet and cook for a minute or two, just until the shrimp are cooked through.
5. Stir in the heavy cream and season the sauce with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste. Remove from heat.
6. In a large pot, bring salted water to a boil. Add the gemelli pasta and cook until al dente, about 10 minutes. Drain well.
7. Place the cooked gemelli in a warm serving dish.
8. Briefly reheat the shrimp and tomato sauce, then pour it over the pasta and toss well to combine.
9. Sprinkle the chopped Italian parsley over the completed dish and serve immediately. Enjoy!
instances.mapper_output: The dish is a shrimp and gemelli pasta with a creamy tomato sauce.
instances.control_output:
**Ingredients:**
- 2 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil
- 1/2 cup finely chopped onion
- 1/2 cup finely chopped fresh fennel bulb
- 1/2 cup red bell pepper, finely chopped
- 1 teaspoon lightly crushed fennel seeds
- 1 cup crushed canned tomatoes
- 1/2 pound shelled medium shrimp
- 1/4 cup heavy cream
- Salt and freshly ground black pepper
- 1/2 pound gemelli pasta
- 1/2 tablespoon finely chopped Italian parsley

**Directions:**
1. Heat the oil in a heavy skillet.
2. Add the onion, chopped fennel, and bell pepper, and sauté until all are tender.
3. Stir in the fennel seeds and the tomatoes.
4. Bring to a simmer and cook for about 15 minutes.
5. Stir in the shrimp and cook for a minute or two longer, just until the shrimp are cooked.
6. Stir in the cream, season the sauce to your taste with salt and pepper, and remove from the heat.
7. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil.
8. Add the gemelli and cook until al dente, about 10 minutes.
9. Drain well.
10. Put the gemelli in a warm serving dish.
11. Briefly reheat the shrimp and the tomato sauce, pour over the pasta, and toss well.
12. Sprinkle the parsley over the completed dish and serve.

**Ingredients:**
- 1 lb hamburger
- 1 cup rice
- 4 cups sliced zucchini
- 1 onion
- 1 can mushroom soup
- 1 cup grated cheese
- Salt & pepper to taste
- Garlic powder to taste

**Directions:**
1. Fry the hamburger with the onion, salt, pepper, and garlic until browned.
2. Add the rice to the mixture and stir well.
3. In a greased 9 x 13 pan, place one layer of zucchini.
4. Add all of the beef and rice mixture on top of the zucchini.
5. Layer the remaining zucchini over the beef and rice mixture.
6. Pour the mushroom soup over the entire dish.
7. Top with grated cheese.
8. Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes or until the cheese is melted and just turning brown.

- 1 onion
- 1 can mushroom soup
- 1 cup grated cheese
- Salt & pepper to taste
- Garlic powder to taste

**Directions:**
1. Fry the hamburger with the onion, salt, pepper, and garlic powder until browned.
2. Add the rice to the mixture and stir well.
3. In a greased 9 x 13 pan, place one layer of zucchini.
4. Spread all of the beef and rice mixture over the zucchini layer.
5. Add the remaining zucchini on top.
6. Pour the mushroom soup over the entire dish.
7. Top with grated cheese.
8. Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes or until the cheese is melted and just turning brown.

instances.inspect_output:
**Apple Pound Cake Recipe**

**Ingredients:**
- 1/2 Apple
- 60 grams Vegetable oil
- 60 grams Sugar
- 1 Egg
- 100 grams Flour
- 3 grams Baking powder
- 1 Milk

**Directions:**
1. Slice the apple into sixths, and then into bite-sized pieces.
2. Preheat the oven to 180°C.
3. Line a pound cake tin with parchment paper or coat it with vegetable oil.
4. Put the vegetable oil, sugar, egg, and apple pieces in a bowl and mix with a whisk.
5. Add the flour, baking powder, and mix well.
6. Sift the powdery ingredients if you have time.
7. Add enough milk to make the batter slightly runny; it should be firmer than pancake batter.
8. Pour the batter into the tin and top it with the apple pieces.
9. Press the apple into the batter with a spoon.
10. Bake in the oven for 25-30 minutes to finish.
11. It will be fluffy when done, but if you leave it overnight, you'll end up with a moist pound cake. Enjoy!
instances.mapper_output: The recipe involves making a fluffy pound cake with apple pieces, which is baked in the oven for 25-30 minutes.
instances.control_output:
**Apple Pound Cake Recipe**

**Ingredients:**
- 1/2 Apple
- 60 grams Vegetable oil
- 60 grams Sugar
- 1 Egg
- 100 grams Flour
- 3 grams Baking powder
- 1 Milk

**Directions:**
1. Slice the apple into sixths, and then into bite-sized pieces.
2. Preheat the oven to 180°C.
3. Line a pound cake tin with parchment paper or coat it with vegetable oil.
4. Put the ingredients marked in a bowl and mix with a whisk.
5. Add the ingredients and mix well.
6. Sift the powdery ingredients if you have time.
7. Add enough milk to make the batter slightly runny; it will be firmer than pancake batter.
8. Pour the batter into the tin and top it with the apple pieces.
9. Press the apple into the batter with a spoon.
10. Bake in the oven for 25-30 minutes to finish.
11. It will be fluffy when done, but if you leave it overnight, you'll end up with a moist pound cake.

The city of Atlanta is considering a new airport, and the mayor wants to use eminent domain to take land from homeowners to build it. The homeowners are fighting the plan. This is a complex issue that requires a thoughtful approach. On one hand, the city needs a new airport to accommodate the growing demand for air travel. On the other hand, using eminent domain to take land from homeowners is a heavy-handed approach that raises concerns about property rights and the impact on local communities.
The issue is not just about the airport, but about the balance between economic development and individual rights. The city should consider alternative solutions, such as working with the homeowners to find a mutually beneficial solution or providing fair compensation for the land taken. The use of eminent domain should be a last resort, and the city should be transparent and accountable in its decision-making process.

hard_prompt: Given an abstract of a paper, generate a title for this paper such that conveys the key focus of the paper.

instances.input: Abstract: There is controversy regarding the appropriate utilization of health care resources in the management of tricyclic antidepressant overdosage. Antidepressant overdose patients presenting to the emergency department (ED) are routinely admitted to intensive care units, but only a small proportion develop cardiac arrhythmias or other complications requiring such an environment. The authors reviewed the findings in 165 patients presenting to an ED with antidepressant overdose. They found that major manifestations of toxicity on ED evaluation (altered mental status, seizures, arrhythmias, and conduction defects) were commonly associated with a complicated hospital course. Patients with the isolated findings of sinus tachycardia or QTc prolongation had no complications. No patient experienced a serious toxic event without major evidence of toxicity on ED evaluation and continued evidence of toxicity during the hospital course. These data support the concept that proper ED evaluation can identify a large body of patients with trivial ingestions who may not require hospital observation.
instances.output: Tricyclic antidepressant overdose: emergency department findings as predictors of clinical course.

instances.inspect_output: The abstract discusses the management of tricyclic antidepressant overdoses in emergency departments (EDs) and the subsequent need for intensive care unit (ICU) admissions. It highlights a study of 165 patients who presented with antidepressant overdoses, revealing that only a small number developed serious complications like cardiac arrhythmias. The authors found that major toxicity indicators, such as altered mental status, seizures, and arrhythmias, were linked to complicated hospital courses, while patients with only sinus tachycardia or QTc prolongation did not experience complications. The findings suggest that thorough evaluation in the ED can help identify patients with minor overdoses who may not need extensive hospital observation, potentially optimizing healthcare resource utilization.
instances.mapper_output: What did the authors find regarding patients with the isolated findings of sinus tachycardia or QTc prolongation?
instances.fs_output: Tricyclic antidepressant overdose: emergency department evaluation and implications for hospital admission.
instances.control_output: The study examines the management of tricyclic antidepressant overdoses in emergency departments (ED) and the necessity of intensive care unit (ICU) admissions. An analysis of 165 patients revealed that while many present with symptoms of toxicity, such as altered mental status, seizures, and arrhythmias, only a small fraction actually require intensive care. Notably, patients exhibiting only sinus tachycardia or QTc prolongation did not experience complications. The findings suggest that thorough evaluation in the ED can effectively identify patients with minor overdoses who may not need prolonged hospital observation, potentially optimizing healthcare resource utilization.
instances.soft2_output: 165 patients with tricyclic antidepressant overdose: clinical presentation and outcome.

instances.input: Abstract: A prospective clinical trial was conducted to estimate the rise in serum glucose level after an intravenous bolus of 50 ml of 50% dextrose solution (D-50) in the emergency department setting. Fifty one subjects with altered levels of consciousness were studied. Of these, 23 patients were known diabetics, and 28 were not diabetic. The change in glucose level for the total study group ranged from a low of 37 mg/dl to a high of 370 mg/dl, with a mean of 166 +/- 77 mg/dl. The mean for the diabetic and non-diabetic groups were 177 +/- 80 mg/dl and 154 +/- 75 mg/dl. These results suggest that serum glucose levels cannot be quantitatively predicted after a single intravenous bolus of D-50.
instances.output: Serum glucose changes after administration of 50% dextrose solution: pre- and in-hospital calculations.

instances.input: Abstract: An unusual case of a misdirected nasogastric tube is described. An elderly woman was brought to an emergency department following intentional drug overdose. Initially unrecognized errant placement of a large-bore nasogastric tube resulted in tension pneumothorax, pneumonia, and subsequent death. Pertinent medical literature is reviewed, clinical considerations for the elderly patient are discussed, and suggestions for proper nasogastric tube placement are offered.
instances.output: Nasogastric intubation: morbidity in an asymptomatic patient.

Title: Misdirected Nasogastric Tube: A Case Study and Clinical Considerations

Abstract: This report details an unusual case involving the misplacement of a nasogastric tube in an elderly woman who was admitted to the emergency department following an intentional drug overdose. The incorrect placement of a large-bore nasogastric tube went initially unrecognized, leading to severe complications including tension pneumothorax, pneumonia, and ultimately, the patient's death. The article reviews relevant medical literature, discusses clinical considerations specific to elderly patients, and provides recommendations for ensuring proper nasogastric tube placement to prevent similar adverse outcomes.

Title: Massive Degloving Injury of the Trunk with Open Pelvic Fracture: A Case Report

Abstract: This report presents a case of a massive degloving injury of the trunk accompanied by an open pelvic fracture and evisceration of abdominal contents resulting from blunt trauma. A notable aspect of this case was the extensive transfusion of 173 units of packed red blood cells and 176 units of fresh frozen plasma within the first thirty hours post-injury. Remarkably, the patient made a full recovery and was able to return to work.

A critical element of the treatment involved the administration of an extraordinary volume of blood products, totaling 173 units of packed red blood cells and 176 units of fresh frozen plasma within the initial thirty hours post-injury. Remarkably, the patient made a full recovery and was able to return to work.

instances.input: Abstract: In the critically ill child, administration of fluids and medications via the intraosseous route often proves life-saving. The authors describe the case of a child with status epilepticus in whom phenytoin was administered via the intraosseous route, and seizure resolution and therapeutic serum levels were achieved. Intraosseous drug administration should be reserved for the rare critically ill child in whom vascular access proves impossible.

instances.input: Abstract: Boerhaave's syndrome represents a diagnostic dilemma for the emergency physician. The prognosis of this truly life-threatening emergency is darkened by any significant diagnostic delay. Unfortunately, classic or expected symptoms and signs are frequently absent at presentation, a circumstance that leads to frequent misdiagnosis. Two cases of Boerhaave's syndrome with "atypical" clinical presentations are reviewed and discussed. It is clear that Boerhaave's syndrome should always be suspected in the evaluation of any sudden chest, abdominal, or back pain associated with emesis. However, emphasis should be placed on the fact that this entity may occur without emesis. The chest radiograph is the most helpful diagnostic aid. Undoubtedly, maintenance of a high degree of suspicion by the emergency physician for Boerhaave's syndrome will lead consistently to earlier diagnosis, and subsequent aggressive intervention should result in considerable reduction in rates of both morbidity and mortality.

instances.input: Abstract: Intraosseous infusions were widely used in pediatric patients during the 1930s and 1940s. Recent reports have re-introduced this concept and confirmed its safety and ready accessability for fluid and drug administration. However, these reports have not addressed the difficulties encountered during insertion of the intraosseous needle. Spinal needles, standard metal intravenous (IV) needles, and bone marrow biopsy needles have been suggested for intraosseous infusion. These needles were tested for ease of insertion on a pediatric cadaver leg. The site for needle placement was also evaluated during the study. It was found that the 13-gauge Kormed/Jamshidi disposable bone marrow/aspiration needle was the easiest to insert and did not plug with bone or tissue during insertion. An area proximal to the medial malleolus was found to provide a stable, relatively flat, and easily penetrable location for needle placement. This method was successfully utilized in ten pediatric and five adult patients. Intraosseous needle placement is a safe, rapid method to gain access to the venous circulation. By utilizing these techniques, a stable, usable fluid line can be established in even the most dehydrated pediatric patients.

instances.inspect_output:
**Title:** Efficacy of Intraosseous Infusion Techniques in Pediatric and Adult Patients

**Abstract:** Intraosseous infusions were widely used in pediatric patients during the 1930s and 1940s. Recent reports have re-introduced this concept and confirmed its safety and ready accessibility for fluid and drug administration. However, these reports have not addressed the difficulties encountered during the insertion of the intraosseous needle. Spinal needles, standard metal intravenous (IV) needles, and bone marrow biopsy needles have been suggested for intraosseous infusion. These needles were tested for ease of insertion on a pediatric cadaver leg. The site for needle placement was also evaluated during the study. It was found that the 13-gauge Kormed/Jamshidi disposable bone marrow/aspiration needle was the easiest to insert and did not plug with bone or tissue during insertion. An area proximal to the medial malleolus was found to provide a stable, relatively flat, and easily penetrable location for needle placement. This method was successfully utilized in ten pediatric and five adult patients. Intraosseous needle placement is a safe, rapid method to gain access to the venous circulation. By utilizing these techniques, a stable, usable fluid line can be established in even the most dehydrated pediatric patients.
instances.mapper_output: What was found to be the easiest needle to insert for intraosseous infusion?
instances.fs_output: Intraosseous infusion techniques: optimizing access in pediatric patients.
instances.control_output: Intraosseous infusions, once common in pediatric care during the 1930s and 1940s, have seen a resurgence due to recent studies confirming their safety and accessibility for administering fluids and medications. However, challenges in inserting intraosseous needles have not been thoroughly addressed in these reports. Various needle types, including spinal needles, standard metal IV needles, and bone marrow biopsy needles, were evaluated for ease of insertion on a pediatric cadaver leg. The study identified the 13-gauge Kormed/Jamshidi disposable bone marrow/aspiration needle as the easiest to insert, with no issues of plugging during the process. The optimal insertion site was found to be proximal to the medial malleolus, which offers a stable and easily penetrable area. This technique was successfully applied in ten pediatric and five adult patients, demonstrating that intraosseous needle placement is a rapid and safe method for accessing venous circulation, particularly beneficial for severely dehydrated pediatric patients.
instances.soft2_output: 13-gauge Kormed/Jamshidi disposable bone marrow/aspiration needle for intraosseous infusion.

instances.input: Abstract: The case of a man who injected turpentine intravenously in an attempt to kill himself is reported. The patient developed immediate pulmonary edema and hypoxia, followed later by cellulitis at the site of injection. Although only one death to date has been attributed to this form of chemical abuse, extensive local reactions, pulmonary involvement, central nervous system depression, and febrile reactions should be anticipated, and such patients should be admitted to the hospital. Patients should be observed for local reactions and myonecrosis around the site of injection, especially as these occur 12-24 hours later.

While there has been only one reported death from this type of chemical abuse, it is important to recognize the potential for severe local reactions, pulmonary issues, central nervous system depression, and febrile responses. Patients who engage in this behavior should be hospitalized for monitoring. Careful observation is necessary for local reactions and myonecrosis, particularly as these complications may arise 12-24 hours post-injection.

instances.input: Abstract: The users of illicit drugs is endangered by both unpredictable concentrations of the drugs and by unexpected adulterants that may be present. Cocaine has been adulterated or admixed with numerous substances, among them local anesthetics, amphetamine, phencyclidine, and strychnine. We report three cases of thallium poisoning following nasal insufflation of a substance that was believed to have been cocaine.

instances.inspect_output: Thallium poisoning has emerged as a significant risk for users of illicit drugs, particularly cocaine, which is often adulterated with various harmful substances. In this report, we present three cases where individuals experienced thallium poisoning after insufflating a substance they believed to be cocaine. This highlights the dangers associated with the unpredictable nature of illicit drug compositions and the potential presence of toxic adulterants.

The report details three cases of thallium poisoning that occurred after individuals insufflated a substance thought to be cocaine.

instances.input: Abstract: The intravenous injection of ethchlorvynol is an uncommon cause of noncardiac pulmonary edema. Two cases of intravenous ethchlorvynol-induced pulmonary edema are presented. The patients fell asleep after injecting the liquid contents of Placydil capsules (ethchlorvynol) and awoke several hours later with severe dyspnea. Arterial blood gases demonstrated marked hypoxia. Chest radiographs revealed bilateral diffuse alveolar densities. The patients' symptoms and radiographic findings resolved after several days of supportive care. Changes in the lung caused by ethchlorvynol may be the result of direct effect of the drug on the lung.
instances.output: Pulmonary edema induced by intravenous ethchlorvynol.

hard_prompt: Given an abstract, generate a keyword (a noun phrase) that best describes the focus or contribution of the paper. Such keywords can be directly from the given abstract or outside it.

instances.input: Abstract: As a means of providing defibrillation as soon as possible for those suffering out-of-hospital ventricular fibrillation, numerous communities have trained and authorized emergency medical technicians (EMTs) to provide defibrillatory shocks (EMT-D). The findings of four controlled studies and the experience of various communities have answered important questions. EMT-D programs have been shown effective in urban, suburban, and rural communities with and without paramedic backup. EMT-D programs appear safe, with errors of commission being extremely rare and errors of omission being of acceptably low incidence. Issues still to be resolved include the degree and amount of training that should be given and whether manual or automatic defibrillators should be used. Medical supervision and quality control is the most important aspect of EMT-D programs. At its core, the success of EMT-D programs is confirmation of the principle of early defibrillation. Wider dissemination of early defibrillation will improve survival from sudden cardiac death.

The findings of four controlled studies and the experience of various communities have answered important questions. EMT-D programs have been shown effective in urban, suburban, and rural communities with and without paramedic backup. EMT-D programs appear safe, with errors of commission being extremely rare and errors of omission being of acceptably low incidence. Issues still to be resolved include the degree and amount of training that should be given and whether manual or automatic defibrillators should be used. Medical supervision and quality control is the most important aspect of EMT-D programs. At its core, the success of EMT-D programs is confirmation of the principle of early defibrillation. Wider dissemination of early defibrillation will improve survival from sudden cardiac death. SN - 0002-9149 UR - http://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/
instances.inspect_output: The abstract discusses the implementation and effectiveness of Emergency Medical Technicians trained to provide defibrillation (EMT-D) in various communities. It highlights findings from controlled studies indicating that EMT-D programs are effective and safe across different settings, including urban, suburban, and rural areas. The abstract notes that while errors in administering defibrillation are rare, there are still unresolved questions regarding training requirements and the choice between manual and automatic defibrillators. It emphasizes the importance of medical supervision and quality control in these programs and concludes that promoting early defibrillation can enhance survival rates from sudden cardiac events.
instances.mapper_output: What are the key findings regarding the effectiveness and safety of EMT-D programs in various communities?
instances.control_output: The abstract discusses the implementation and effectiveness of Emergency Medical Technicians trained to provide defibrillation (EMT-D) in various communities. It highlights findings from controlled studies indicating that EMT-D programs are effective and safe across different settings, including urban, suburban, and rural areas, regardless of paramedic support. The text notes that while errors in administering defibrillation are rare, there are still unresolved questions regarding training requirements and the choice between manual and automatic defibrillators. The importance of medical supervision and quality control in these programs is emphasized, reinforcing the principle that early defibrillation is crucial for improving survival rates from sudden cardiac events.

instances.input: Abstract: There is controversy regarding the appropriate utilization of health care resources in the management of tricyclic antidepressant overdosage. Antidepressant overdose patients presenting to the emergency department (ED) are routinely admitted to intensive care units, but only a small proportion develop cardiac arrhythmias or other complications requiring such an environment. The authors reviewed the findings in 165 patients presenting to an ED with antidepressant overdose. They found that major manifestations of toxicity on ED evaluation (altered mental status, seizures, arrhythmias, and conduction defects) were commonly associated with a complicated hospital course. Patients with the isolated findings of sinus tachycardia or QTc prolongation had no complications. No patient experienced a serious toxic event without major evidence of toxicity on ED evaluation and continued evidence of toxicity during the hospital course. These data support the concept that proper ED evaluation can identify a large body of patients with trivial ingestions who may not require hospital observation.

instances.inspect_output: The abstract discusses the management of tricyclic antidepressant overdoses in emergency departments (ED). It highlights the debate over the necessity of intensive care unit admissions for these patients, noting that only a small number develop serious complications. A review of 165 patients revealed that significant toxicity indicators, such as altered mental status, seizures, and arrhythmias, were linked to complicated hospital stays. Conversely, patients with only sinus tachycardia or QTc prolongation did not face complications. The findings suggest that thorough ED evaluations can help identify patients with minor overdoses who may not need extended hospital observation.
instances.mapper_output: What did the authors find regarding the relationship between major manifestations of toxicity and the hospital course of patients with antidepressant overdose?
instances.control_output: The study examines the management of tricyclic antidepressant overdoses in emergency departments (ED) and the necessity of intensive care unit (ICU) admissions. Analyzing 165 cases, the authors found that while many patients exhibited major toxicity signs—such as altered mental status, seizures, and arrhythmias—only a few required ICU care. Notably, patients with only sinus tachycardia or QTc prolongation did not face complications. The findings suggest that thorough ED evaluations can effectively identify patients with minor overdoses who may not need extended hospital observation, challenging the routine practice of ICU admissions for all overdose cases.

hard_prompt: In this task you will be given a claim and a perspective. You should determine whether that perspective supports or undermines the claim. If the perspective could possibly convince someone with different view, it is supporting, otherwise it is undermining.

instances.input:
claim: Children should not be allowed to inherit vast wealth as this damages them and society.
 perspective: Inherited wealth demotivates the recipients so that they put less effort into training, education and social skills.
instances.soft_output: assistant
instances.inspect_output: The claim suggests that allowing children to inherit vast wealth can have negative consequences for both the individuals and society as a whole. The perspective provided supports this claim by arguing that inherited wealth can lead to a lack of motivation among recipients. When children receive large sums of money without having to work for it, they may feel less inclined to invest effort into their personal development, such as training, education, and acquiring social skills. This demotivation can result in a generation that is less prepared to contribute positively to society, potentially leading to broader social issues such as inequality and a lack of innovation. Therefore, the argument emphasizes the importance of earning one's wealth to foster a sense of responsibility and drive, which can ultimately benefit both the individual and society.
instances.control_output: Inherited wealth can create a sense of entitlement among children, leading them to believe that they do not need to work hard or develop their skills. This lack of motivation can result in a diminished drive for personal growth, education, and social interaction. As they rely on their inherited wealth, they may miss out on valuable life experiences that come from striving for success and overcoming challenges. Furthermore, this can have broader societal implications, as a generation of unmotivated individuals may contribute less to the economy and community, perpetuating a cycle of inequality and reducing overall societal progress. Therefore, limiting the inheritance of vast wealth could encourage children to pursue their own paths, fostering a more dynamic and engaged society.

instances.input:
claim: Domestic intelligence agencies have a legitimate role to play in democracy.
 perspective: The government does not have the right to spy on its citizens
instances.soft_output: assistant undermine support
instances.inspect_output: The claim suggests that domestic intelligence agencies can contribute positively to the functioning of a democracy by ensuring national security and protecting citizens from threats. This perspective emphasizes the importance of oversight and accountability, arguing that while intelligence agencies may have a role, they should not infringe on the privacy and rights of individuals. The tension between security and civil liberties is central to this debate, highlighting the need for a balance that respects citizens' rights while maintaining safety.
instances.control_output:
The claim that domestic intelligence agencies have a legitimate role to play in democracy suggests that these agencies are necessary for maintaining national security, preventing crime, and protecting citizens. Proponents argue that intelligence gathering can help identify threats and ensure the safety of the public, which is a fundamental aspect of a functioning democracy.

On the other hand, the perspective that the government does not have the right to spy on its citizens raises concerns about privacy, civil liberties, and government overreach. Critics argue that surveillance can lead to abuses of power, erosion of trust between citizens and the state, and the potential for discrimination and targeting of specific groups.

This presents a tension between the need for security and the protection of individual rights. Balancing these interests is crucial in a democratic society, where both the safety of the public and the rights of individuals must be respected.

instances.input:
claim: Should Turkey Be Accepted In The EU.
 perspective: As a matter of precedent, Turkey should be allowed into the EU.
instances.soft_output: assistant undermine support undermine
instances.inspect_output:
The argument for accepting Turkey into the EU based on precedent can be supported by several points:

1. **Historical Precedent**: The EU has previously accepted countries with diverse cultural, religious, and political backgrounds. For instance, countries like Greece, Spain, and Portugal were admitted despite having different governance systems and histories. Accepting Turkey would be consistent with this precedent of inclusivity.

2. **Geopolitical Considerations**: Turkey's strategic location at the crossroads of Europe and Asia makes it a significant player in regional stability. Its membership could enhance the EU's influence in the Middle East and Central Asia, aligning with historical precedents where the EU has expanded its borders for strategic reasons.

3. **Economic Integration**: Turkey has a growing economy and a young population, which could contribute positively to the EU's economic landscape. Previous enlargements have shown that integrating new members can lead to economic growth for both the EU and the new member states.

4. **Democratic Progress**: While Turkey has faced challenges regarding democracy and human rights, the EU has historically engaged with countries to promote reforms. Accepting Turkey could provide a platform for further democratic development, similar to how the EU has supported reforms in Eastern European countries.

5. **Cultural Exchange**: Turkey's diverse culture and history can enrich the EU's cultural tapestry. The precedent of cultural integration in the EU suggests that diversity can be a strength rather than a barrier.

In conclusion, based on historical precedents of inclusivity, strategic importance, economic potential, and cultural enrichment, Turkey should be considered for EU membership.
instances.control_output: Turkey should be accepted into the EU based on the principle of precedent. Historically, the EU has expanded to include countries that have met certain political, economic, and social criteria. Allowing Turkey to join would be consistent with the EU's commitment to inclusivity and the promotion of democratic values. Furthermore, Turkey's strategic location and its role as a bridge between Europe and Asia could enhance the EU's geopolitical influence and economic ties. By accepting Turkey, the EU would also reinforce its stance on fostering stability and cooperation in the region.

instances.input:
claim: Arming the police is a good idea.
 perspective: arming police will not help violence
instances.soft_output: assistant
instances.inspect_output: The perspective that "arming police will not help violence" challenges the claim that "arming the police is a good idea." This perspective suggests that increasing the armament of police forces may not effectively reduce violence and could potentially exacerbate the situation. It implies that simply providing police with more weapons does not address the root causes of violence and may lead to more confrontations or escalate tensions between law enforcement and the community. Therefore, the effectiveness of arming police in reducing violence is questioned, indicating that alternative approaches may be necessary to ensure public safety and community trust.
instances.control_output: The perspective that arming police will not help reduce violence suggests that increasing the availability of firearms to law enforcement may not address the root causes of violence and could potentially escalate confrontations. Critics argue that more weapons can lead to more aggressive policing and may increase the likelihood of violent encounters between police and civilians. Instead of arming police, they advocate for alternative approaches such as community engagement, de-escalation training, and addressing social issues that contribute to violence. This perspective emphasizes the importance of finding solutions that prioritize safety and trust within communities rather than relying on increased militarization of the police force.

instances.input:
claim: Same sex relationships should have the same legal status as heterosexual relationships under EU law.
 perspective: There are economic advantages for states to approve gay marriages.
instances.soft_output: assistantunsupported
instances.inspect_output:
The claim that same-sex relationships should have the same legal status as heterosexual relationships under EU law is supported by the perspective that there are economic advantages for states to approve gay marriages. Legal recognition of same-sex marriages can lead to increased economic activity through various channels. For instance, legalizing gay marriage can boost the wedding industry, as couples will spend on venues, catering, and other wedding-related services. Additionally, it can attract tourism, as many people may choose to travel to countries where their relationships are recognized and celebrated.

Moreover, granting legal status to same-sex relationships can enhance the overall economic stability of families, as it allows for shared benefits such as health insurance, tax benefits, and inheritance rights. This can lead to a more financially secure population, which in turn can contribute positively to the economy. Furthermore, inclusive policies can improve a country's international reputation, making it more attractive for foreign investment and skilled workers who value equality and non-discrimination.

In summary, recognizing same-sex relationships legally not only promotes equality and human rights but also brings tangible economic benefits to states, making a strong case for their equal status under EU law.
instances.control_output: Same-sex relationships should have the same legal status as heterosexual relationships under EU law because there are significant economic advantages for states that approve gay marriages. Legal recognition of same-sex marriages can lead to increased economic activity through wedding-related spending, tourism, and the creation of jobs in various sectors such as hospitality and event planning. Additionally, granting equal legal status can enhance the overall well-being of LGBTQ+ individuals, leading to a more productive workforce and reduced healthcare costs associated with mental health issues stemming from discrimination. By embracing equality in marriage laws, EU states can foster a more inclusive society that not only respects human rights but also reaps the economic benefits of a diverse and thriving population.

instances.input:
claim: Same sex relationships should have the same legal status as heterosexual relationships under EU law.
 perspective: Legalizing gay marriage is a stepping stone toward disgusting and harmful marriage practices such as incestuous, bestial and child marriages.
instances.soft_output: undermine support undermine support
instances.inspect_output: The claim advocates for equal legal recognition of same-sex relationships under EU law, emphasizing the importance of equality and non-discrimination. In contrast, the perspective presented argues against this claim by suggesting that legalizing same-sex marriage could lead to a slippery slope of accepting other forms of marriage that are considered harmful or immoral. This perspective relies on fear-based reasoning rather than addressing the core issue of equality and human rights for same-sex couples. It is important to focus on the rights and dignity of individuals in same-sex relationships, rather than allowing unfounded fears to dictate legal recognition.
instances.control_output:
The perspective presented argues that legalizing same-sex marriage could lead to the acceptance of other forms of marriage that are considered harmful or unethical, such as incest, bestiality, and child marriage. This viewpoint is often rooted in a slippery slope argument, suggesting that allowing one change in legal recognition could lead to further changes that society may find unacceptable. 

However, it is important to note that many legal systems, including those in the EU, have safeguards and criteria that distinguish between consensual adult relationships and those that involve exploitation or harm. The legalization of same-sex marriage is based on principles of equality, human rights, and the recognition of love and commitment between consenting adults, rather than a precedent for harmful practices. 

The argument against same-sex marriage often relies on fear and misconceptions rather than evidence. Many countries that have legalized same-sex marriage have not seen a rise in the practices mentioned in the perspective. Instead, they have seen positive social outcomes, including increased acceptance and support for LGBTQ+ individuals. 

In conclusion, while the perspective raises concerns about potential consequences, it does not take into account the legal and ethical frameworks that govern marriage and relationships, nor the positive impacts of recognizing same-sex relationships as equal under the law.

instances.inspect_output: The claim that the use of child performers should be banned is supported by the perspective that being a performer can make the child physically vulnerable. Child performers often face unique challenges and pressures that can impact their physical and emotional well-being. The demanding nature of performance work, including long hours, intense schedules, and the need to meet high expectations, can lead to physical strain and exhaustion. Additionally, the environment in which they work may expose them to risks that are not suitable for their age, potentially compromising their safety and health. Therefore, banning the use of child performers could be a necessary step to protect their physical vulnerability and ensure their overall well-being.
instances.control_output: The use of child performers should be banned because being a performer can make the child physically vulnerable. Child performers often face intense pressure and scrutiny, which can lead to physical and emotional stress. The demanding schedules and environments in the entertainment industry can expose them to various risks, including exploitation and abuse. Additionally, the physical demands of performing can take a toll on their developing bodies, leading to potential long-term health issues. Protecting children from these vulnerabilities is essential, and banning their participation in performance roles is a necessary step to ensure their safety and well-being.

instances.input:
claim: UK should have a second Brexit referendum before leaving the EU.
 perspective: The referendum is inconsistent with the UK's tradition of representative democracy  EU membersh
instances.soft_output: assistant undermine support
instances.inspect_output: The claim suggests that the UK should hold a second referendum on Brexit before finalizing its departure from the EU. However, the perspective argues that this idea is inconsistent with the UK's tradition of representative democracy. In representative democracy, elected officials make decisions on behalf of the citizens, and the initial Brexit referendum was a direct expression of the public's will. Holding a second referendum could undermine the authority of the elected representatives and the decision made in the first referendum, suggesting a potential conflict between direct democracy and representative governance.
instances.control_output: The argument against a second Brexit referendum is rooted in the principles of representative democracy that the UK has upheld for centuries. In a representative democracy, elected officials are entrusted with making decisions on behalf of their constituents, reflecting the will of the people through their votes in Parliament. A second referendum could undermine this system by suggesting that the initial decision made by the electorate is not valid or worthy of respect. It raises questions about the stability and integrity of democratic processes, as frequent referendums could lead to a cycle of uncertainty and division, rather than allowing elected representatives to govern effectively based on the mandate they received from the public. Thus, the call for a second referendum may be seen as inconsistent with the UK's democratic traditions.

instances.inspect_output: The claim that everyone should automatically be opted into an organ donation scheme is supported by the perspective that many individuals, despite their support for organ donation, fail to take the necessary steps to register as donors. This suggests that an automatic opt-in system could significantly increase the number of registered donors, thereby potentially saving more lives. By removing the barrier of registration, we can ensure that more people are included in the organ donation pool, addressing the critical shortage of available organs for transplantation.
instances.control_output: The claim that everyone should automatically be opted into an organ donation scheme addresses a significant issue in public health and organ availability. The perspective highlights a common challenge: many individuals who support organ donation do not take the necessary steps to register as donors. This suggests that an automatic opt-in system could potentially increase the number of registered donors, thereby saving more lives. By making organ donation the default option, we could overcome the apathy or procrastination that often prevents people from registering, ensuring that more individuals are available to donate their organs when needed. This approach could lead to a more robust organ donation system, ultimately benefiting society as a whole.

instances.input:
claim: Marriage is an outdated institution.
 perspective: Those who are observant religiously think that marriage is important.
instances.soft_output: assistant undermine support undermine
instances.inspect_output: The claim that "marriage is an outdated institution" suggests a viewpoint that marriage no longer holds the same significance or relevance in modern society. However, the perspective provided highlights that for those who are observant religiously, marriage is still considered an important and meaningful commitment. This indicates a divergence in beliefs about marriage, where religious individuals may view it as a sacred and essential part of life, contrasting with the idea that it is outdated. This difference in perspective underscores the complexity of the institution of marriage and how its value can vary greatly depending on cultural, religious, and personal beliefs.
instances.control_output: While some may view marriage as an outdated institution, many observant religious individuals believe it holds significant importance. For them, marriage is not just a social contract but a sacred covenant that reflects their values and beliefs. It serves as a foundation for family life, community stability, and spiritual growth. Thus, the perspective that marriage is outdated overlooks the deep cultural and religious significance it continues to hold for many people.
